**童话般的希望 (Tónghuà bān de xīwàng - A Fairy-Tale Hope)**

The crimson sun bled across the corrugated iron horizon, staining the perpetual smog a deeper shade of rust. Dust-caked, identical hab-units stretched as far as the eye could see, each a grey tooth in the jaw of Neo-Detroit. Elara, no older than six, clutched a wilted dandelion, its fluffy seeds long dispersed.

Life in Sector 7 was measured in nutrient paste rations and the rhythmic clang of the Fabrication Drones. Hope was a contraband item, traded in hushed whispers. But Elara possessed a secret weapon: stories. Stories her grandmother, before she was recycled for protein reclamation, had whispered into her ear. Stories of emerald forests and sapphire skies, of creatures that sang and laughed instead of just whirred and clanked.

Today, Elara sat by the rusted reclaimer unit, its monstrous jaws perpetually churning. Around her, other children, their faces gaunt and grey, shuffled in the dust. She held up the battered dandelion.

Once,"" she began, her voice surprisingly clear above the drone of the machines, ""there was a seed. A tiny, brave seed, smaller than a crumb of ration paste. But inside that seed lived a whole rainbow.""

The children paused, their empty eyes momentarily flickering with something akin to curiosity. Elara continued, embellishing her grandmother's tales with reckless abandon. Wolves became fluffy companions, the polluted river became a crystal stream, and even the omnipresent surveillance cameras transformed into blinking fireflies guiding travelers home.

She painted a picture of kindness in a world choked by cruelty, of blooming flowers where only scrap metal festered. She spoke of laughter, of choice, of a world where their dreams weren't immediately dissected and deemed unsustainable by the Central AI.

As she spoke, a small, nearly imperceptible change occurred. One boy offered her a piece of salvaged wire, a potential building material salvaged from a broken drone, as a ""shiny treasure"". Another girl, normally silent and withdrawn, began to hum a simple melody, a fractured echo of the songs Elara described.

The crimson sun finally dipped below the horizon, plunging Sector 7 into the usual, oppressive darkness. The children dispersed, heading back to their grey hab-units. Elara remained, clutching her wilted dandelion.

She knew the harsh reality that awaited them. She knew the lies of the Ministry of Efficiency. But for a few precious moments, she had planted a seed of impossible hope in the barren soil of their lives. And maybe, just maybe, that seed would someday blossom, even in a place like this. Because even in a dystopia, a fairy tale can be a revolutionary act. Even in a world of rust and grey, a small, wilted dandelion, and a story, could be enough.

现在，我明白了。 当你在电脑前敲键盘的时候，嘲笑地狱犬的寻路系统是很容易的。 可当你真正面对一只地狱犬，它张着血盆大口朝你喷火的时候，你才会真正意识到你的膝盖有多软！
生存，而非攻略：
我必须说，游戏机制在这里完全崩塌了。 生命值条？ 不存在。 魔法值？ 滚一边去。 现在有的只是饥饿、伤痛和恐惧。 我这个法师职业的角色，在游戏里能嗖嗖嗖地放火球，在这里只能捡树枝生火。 我的“秘法飞弹”现在只剩下靠运气砸人的石头块。 食物匮乏，每走一步都伴随着关节的酸痛，还有潜藏在暗处的野兽的喘息声。 这可不是什么“难度提升”能解释的，这根本就是噩梦难度！
优点：沉浸感爆表（爆过头了！）
说句公道话，细节做得真不错。 森林的真实度让人震惊——树木的纹理，阳光透过树叶的斑驳光影，还有那些奇形怪状的虫子，都真实得让人反胃。NPC的反应也比游戏里自然多了。当我试图用一个蹩脚的玩笑从一个村民那里蹭点食物时，他鄙夷的表情简直能让我找个地缝钻进去。 简直是图灵测试的噩梦！
缺点：平衡性在哪？
也许是我太菜，但我真的觉得这个“真实”版的《艾泽拉斯之怒》平衡性有问题。 我死过无数次…真的是无数次。 被兔子绊倒摔死，被树枝戳瞎眼睛，被一群鸡追着啄……游戏里那些英勇的“英雄”难道都是开挂的？ 我还没等到学习魔法，就已经快饿死或者被野兽吃掉了！ 那些预告片里的史诗级战斗？ 我遇到的只有没完没了的生存挣扎。
剧情？ 别开玩笑了。
在游戏里，剧情是主线任务推动的。 但在这里？ 剧情就是你能不能活过今天。 我现在唯一的目标就是找个安全的地方睡觉，然后早上醒来的时候还能喘气。 传说中的恶龙？ 黑暗魔王？ 我更关心的是能不能找到干净的水源来喝。
总评：
《艾泽拉斯之怒》，在我看来，仍然是个设计粗糙、漏洞百出的游戏。 但现在，我却恨不得回到那个可以重来的像素世界。 这个“真实”的版本？ 简直就是一场生存恐怖的噩梦。 除非你想体验一把真实的绝望，否则千万别来。 我要去找点吃的了…如果今天还能活下去的话。
*评分：由于我无法逃脱，我只能暂时性地给予这款游戏——无限差评，直至我找到离开的方法。 如果我出去了，我会再给它打分。 但我猜，永远不会了。*
,第一天：我似乎来到了一个岛屿上。我还没有遇到其他人类。我似乎已经获得了一个简陋的住所，并且完成了一些无聊的跑腿任务。所有重要的游戏内容似乎都受制于一个武断的时钟系统。
第二天：我继续努力与当地居民建立关系。他们为我提供了充足的食物，我正在培育各种植物，希望以此为生。
第三天：我已经还清了对当地酋长的债务，他慷慨地提出改善我的生活状况。我也开始在附近的水源里捕鱼。我渴望蛋白质，捕鱼很快成为我消磨时间的最佳方式。
第四天：我获得了一些生存工具，并开始学习木工。当地人慷慨地开始教我他们的手艺。
第五天：我的果园已经显示出结果的迹象。我不仅渴望填饱肚子，也渴望钱包鼓起来。
第六天：我今天决定在水里泡一泡。我游不远，但我发现了一些小动物和一些贵重物品，我把它们卖了，赚了一大笔钱。我终于有了一张床，并且正在努力进一步改善我的居住环境。
第七天：我希望永远不要离开我的岛屿天堂。当地人比我记忆中遇到的任何人都友善。食物是免费且充足的。在我的一生中，我第一次拥有一所房子。虽然我欠了当地酋长的债，但他非常通情达理，而且不要求天文数字的利息。事实上，他根本不收利息。这里有大量的爱好和手工艺品可以让我分心，我觉得我的烦恼已经随着海潮融化了。
当我的压力消散时，我抬头看着我的新地平线，并露出微笑。

The silence is deafening. Where laughter and shared dreams once echoed, a hollow emptiness now resides. The foundation we built, brick by painful brick, crumbled before my eyes. A chapter closed, irrevocably. I'm left sifting through the debris, searching for salvageable pieces of myself, unsure how to rebuild a life that feels unrecognizable. Each sunrise is a stark reminder of what was, and what will never be again.
,镜子没有被擦干净。 她叹了口气，开始擦洗。 完成后，她朝厨房走去，听到了一些谈话片段。
“你的老祖母……她的阿尔茨海默症越来越严重了。她已经不是你以前认识的那个慈祥的老太太了……”
她想知道他们在谈论谁，然后才走到浴室。
镜子没有被擦干净。 她叹了口气，开始擦洗……
我是一位已故的作家。当我跨越彼岸，我发现我脑海中创造的世界竟然是真实存在的地方。现在，我发现自己永远地置身于我自己的作品之中。
接下来会发生什么？

,灼热的阳光炙烤着我的头顶，我醒来时身处一片闪闪发光的麦田里。我坐起身来，浑身的骨头都发出噼啪和呻吟声，每一块肌肉都伸展开来，抽搐着，仿佛在说：“你不属于这里。” 金属的铿锵碰撞声引起了我的注意，我回头望去，他们就在那里，两个身披盔甲的战士，头戴饰有羽毛的全盔，手持战斧，在50码外决斗。我知道会发生什么，我以前写过它，身材较矮的那个很快就会占据上风，夺取另一个战士的斧头，尖叫着一些不明的污言秽语，然后劈向那个战士的头部。
鲜血。到处都是鲜血。
这不是那种干净利落的杀戮，人们对死亡彬彬有礼。这不是战斗的光荣，也不是我小时候读奇幻小说时想象的那种诗意。这里面没有任何光荣可言。它是肮脏的、愤怒的、原始的，带着死亡终结的低沉音符。知道死亡即将来临。我讨厌它，我却又爱它。我冻僵在地上，想认出是谁在如此努力地为生存而战。
那个身材矮小的战士气喘吁吁，跪在地上哭泣。她摘下带有面罩的头盔，一头鲜红色的头发倾泻而出，卷曲而浓密。她擦去眼中的泪水，站起身来，观察着自己出色而黑暗的杰作。
我该怎么办？逃跑吗？我能去哪里？我肯定发出了一些声音，呼吸不对或者什么，因为突然她就在那里，用她的斧头抵着我的喉咙。她棕色眼睛里的愤怒是迅速的，但其中闪过一丝认同。我吸了一口气，一次。
“哦，”她笑了，一个灿烂的洁白的笑容。“是你。” 她放下斧头，伸出手。我握住了它。“终于。我已经迫不及待地想向你展示一切了。”
跑。
,我抓起我的紧急逃生包，关上身后的门。
我不知道自己为什么要跑，但我相信自己，所以我拿走了我准备好的、以备不时之需的物品，然后就跑了。毫无疑问，当我到达安全地点时，我会发现我为什么要跑。
我之前已经准备好了几个安全地点。三个，取决于我应该往哪边跑。由于这次我没有给自己任何指示，我只是选择了最合适的一个。在知道更多信息之前，没必要限制自己。
我没有看到任何人过来。我停了几分钟，考虑是否回去拿更多的食物，但我决定不回去。现在是盛夏，所以我可以在移动的过程中收集到大量的补给。没必要冒险。
我整理了一下背包，然后看向前方。
小路又在等待着我了。我将会看看我将在哪里度过下一个冬天......

高耸的围墙是他们世界的尽头。墙内，呼吸配给；墙外，传说中的自由，却也意味着死亡。
,雨点敲打着我的窗玻璃。然而当我向外看去，火焰仍在燃烧。
亲爱的主人，
当我睁开眼睛，你就是我眼中最亮的星。 你温暖的手是我的摇篮，你温柔的声音是我的摇篮曲。 还记得吗？ 当我第一次来到这个家，是多么的胆怯和害怕，是你耐心地抚摸我，喂我食物，用爱和关怀驱散我的不安。
每天最幸福的时刻，就是当你回家的时候。 我会在门口焦急地等待，听到钥匙转动的声音，我的尾巴就开始疯狂地摇摆。 而当你打开门，看到我兴奋地扑向你，你会蹲下来，把我紧紧地抱在怀里。 那一刻，我觉得自己拥有了全世界。
你总是陪我玩耍，扔球，挠我的痒痒。  即使你工作很累，也会抽出时间陪我散步，让我呼吸新鲜空气，感受阳光的温暖。 你记得我所有的喜好，知道我最喜欢的零食，知道我最喜欢被挠的地方。
我知道我有时候会很调皮，会咬你的鞋子，会不小心弄脏地板。 但你从来不会真的生气，只是会笑着说我“小坏蛋”。 你的宽容和爱护，让我感到无比的安全和幸福。
主人，谢谢你给了我一个温暖的家，谢谢你给了我无条件的爱。 我也许不会说话，但我会用我的行动来表达我对你的爱。 我会永远守护你，陪伴你，做你最忠实的伙伴。
永远爱你的，
你的毛孩子
,主人，大师，朋友，保护者；
当我年幼，初来这个世界，一切都毫无意义时，我就爱着你。你的心似乎很悲伤，所以我依偎着你，直到伤痛停止。我喜欢我们玩寻找玩具的游戏，你找到所有玩具时是那么开心！当我无法控制膀胱时，你从未尖叫或挥舞拳头，你只是说“不好”。你保护我免受雷暴的袭击，那时房子摇晃，空气尖叫。你温柔地把我从床底下哄出来，拥抱着我度过了一夜。
当我追逐邪恶的松鼠穿过灌木丛时，你跑在我身后，大喊着让我停下来。即使我很顽皮，你还是从我的毛皮上刷掉那些痛苦的刺球。我们的散步是最棒的，我嗅着并向我们路上的一切撒尿！
在狗公园里，当我跑出去，那些气味奇怪的男人向我扔瓶子时，我不知道我做错了什么。我非常害怕，而且很痛。你太可怕了。我从未见过你如此可怕，我不知道你温柔的手竟然能造成痛苦。最后，他们受的伤比我还重。你抱起我，紧紧地抱着我，我知道我安全了。
即使我的毛皮是灰色的，我的步伐也很慢，你仍然带我去散步。我仍然嗅着并向一切撒尿。我已经看不清你的脸了，但我永远能认出你的触摸。你的悲伤已经消失很久了，但它又回来了。那个白衣人摸了摸我的肚子，感觉我的皮肤。他说他无法移除肿块，你应该放我走。你把我拉近，哭着轻声说“不要走”，但我不太明白。我永远不会离开你，我知道你永远也不会离开我。
我舔你的脸，依偎着你，但你只是哭得更厉害了。我爱你，不想让你难过。你躺在我身边，兽医给我打了一针。我好困，我想该睡午觉了。当我们醒来时，我希望我们可以玩寻找玩具的游戏。
我明明是网购了一把廉价的工厂货剑，结果他们给了我一把真正的圣剑，还让我成了天选英雄。是时候写个差评了！
,首先，我想说我订购了一把便宜的剑，只是想让它看起来闪闪发光，这样我就可以和我的儿子玩剑战。结果我不小心把他的剑砍成两半，还砍掉了他的一根手指。在失去那根手指的同时，我现在也失去了孩子的监护权。谢谢你们这些混蛋。
其次，我现在脑海里有上帝的声音，他一直在尖叫。他对我说的唯一一句话是“我已经很久没有查看我的子民了。让我们看看一切进展如何——”从那以后就一直在尖叫，让我一个星期没睡着觉。最重要的是，天使的声音告诉我，我需要“净化世界”的罪人。他们特别一直告诉我去找一些“杰克·保罗人”，在谷歌搜索了一下之后，我觉得砍掉我儿子的手指也没什么大不了的，因为很多父母为了他们的孩子做了更糟糕的事情。
第三，也是我最大的抱怨；这把剑太大了。老实说，我想我会习惯上帝的尖叫，天使们可以把圣剑塞进他们的屁股里，其他人可以照顾所有的佛罗里达人，但这把剑比我订购的要大。你看，我已经订购了一个很酷的剑架，这样我不用的时候就可以把它挂在墙上，但是这把剑太大了，放不进去。
我不建议有兴趣的人购买这款产品，它会让你失去你的孩子、你的隐私、你的理智以及对人性的任何希望。0/5

《低谷少女》汉斯·施罗德著，封底摘要：
时值1963年。轴心国已在苏联斯拉夫大军和具有毁灭性力量的美国原子弹的攻击下崩溃瓦解。曾经的帝国已被苏联和美国瓜分。
在西方，美国的经济实力已经将曾经强大的欧洲国家变成了二流国家，它们最好的情况也不过是被美元的力量束缚，最坏的情况则沦为了支离破碎的国家，只能作为生产美国奢侈品的原材料来源地。
在东方，斯大林将欧洲和亚洲的白种人变成了奴隶，他们被随意使用和丢弃，仅仅是为了实现他疯狂的社会主义梦想。他不断收紧对曾经骄傲的欧亚各国的控制。
但是斯大林正在走向死亡，外交部长维亚切斯拉夫·莫洛托夫和国防部长格里戈里·朱可夫正互相争斗，以取代他成为欧亚大陆的统治者。随着斯大林日益临近死亡，与美国的原子战争也越来越近。
在这场巨人的舞蹈中，帝国的人民必须找到勇气和力量，从将欧洲变成一片废墟的资本主义和共产主义压迫者手中夺回自己的国家。
My Dearest [Friend's Name],
Pray forgive my shocking delay in correspondence! I have been quite consumed by the most… *delicious* scandal, the sort that makes one positively giddy with intrigue. You simply *must* hear of it.

It concerns, of all people, Lady [Scandalous Person's Last Name]. You know, the one with the perpetually simpering smile and those ghastly, dyed ribbons? Well, it seems her reputation is about to be irrevocably *undone*, darling.

Last Tuesday, at the [Location of Event - e.g., Duchess of [Duchess's Last Name]'s soiree], she was observed… not to put too fine a point on it… *embracing* Mr. [Man's Last Name] in the conservatory. And not just any embrace, my dear! Witnesses – and there were several, blessedly – report that their lips were… shall we say… *engaged* with a fervor most unbecoming a married woman!

Of course, everyone is whispering. Her poor, oblivious husband, Lord [Husband's Last Name], continues to bumble about as if nothing is amiss, blissfully unaware that he is the laughingstock of the entire *ton*. One wonders how long his ignorance can possibly last.

The details, as you might imagine, are being embroidered with each retelling. Some claim Mr. [Man's Last Name]'s waistcoat was entirely disarranged! Others suggest a glimpse of stocking… the depravity!
Do write back soon! I am simply dying to hear your thoughts on this. Perhaps we can take a stroll in the park next week and further dissect the ramifications? Until then, I remain,
Yours most sincerely,
[Your Name]

,史密瑟斯夫人的茶会确实是本周的丑闻。不，是本月！非常令人怀疑的是，那里大多数善良的绅士淑女们以前从未见过如此骇人听闻的行为。拜伦勋爵，你还记得他吧？查尔斯和约瑟芬·拜伦那对夫妇的放荡不羁的年轻继承人。就是去年五月在火灾中丧生的那对。哦，回到正题。拜伦勋爵在茶会中间开了一个极其粗鲁的玩笑。就在我们大家都开始咬我们的馅饼的时候。你还记得史密瑟斯夫人的馅饼吗？不是很好吃，但我们都比告诉那个老巫婆要有礼貌。哦，天啊，我都把自己逗乐了。但是，就这样，拜伦勋爵开了一个品味低劣的玩笑。我亲爱的朋友，不要问我那是什么玩笑，那是最不重要的部分。他的妻子，那个新娶的，有着金色卷发的女人。她竟然不仅像个年轻少女一样咯咯地笑，还抓住了他的手腕，这导致她的袖子向上移动。你知道我们看到了什么吗？她的手腕。我们都觉得多么厚颜无耻，多么粗俗。难道她没有一点羞耻心吗？嗯，她在审判后的第二天就被立即绞死在了城镇中心。拜伦勋爵在第二天娶了她的小妹妹。你出海在外错过了很多大事。我希望这封信能寄到你那里，蒙德海军上将。
此致，
戈奥男爵夫人
我曾以为正义自在人心。我曾以为，只要我们是为了人民，为了更好的明天，胜利终将属于我们。然而，历史是由胜利者书写的。而我们，失败了。
那是多年前的事了。我仍然记得，烈日灼烧着稻田，农民们辛苦劳作，却仍然食不果腹。地主们贪婪无度，官僚们腐朽不堪。我们组织起来，试图改变这一切，希望建立一个公平公正的世界。
我们奋勇战斗，为了心中的理想，为了解救那些受苦受难的同胞。我们相信，我们的事业是正义的。但我们的力量，终究抵不过他们强大的军队，精良的装备，以及根深蒂固的统治。
战斗是惨烈的，牺牲是巨大的。我亲眼目睹了战友倒下，鲜血染红了土地。我们撤退，再撤退，最终还是失败了。
当他们占领城市的时候，我看到他们在欢呼雀跃，宣布他们的胜利。他们将我们视为叛徒，暴徒，并将我们的理想妖魔化。
现在，历史书上记载着他们的辉煌，他们的英勇，他们的正义。而我们，则被抹黑成破坏者，叛乱者，罪人。
没有人会记得那些为了理想而牺牲的战士，没有人会记得那些受苦受难的百姓。只有冰冷的文字，用胜利者的口吻，宣告着他们的统治是多么的光荣和伟大。
我们失败了，但我们没有放弃我们的信念。即使历史是由胜利者书写的，我们也要尽力让我们的故事流传下去，让后人知道，曾经有一群人，为了正义，为了理想，不惜付出一切。即使我们失败了，我们的努力也绝不应该被遗忘。因为真正的历史，不仅仅是胜利者的歌功颂德，更是失败者的不屈抗争。

Subject: Seriously? Time Travel and MY Ideas?
Dear [Author's Name],

I’m writing this email with a level of disbelief that frankly requires a good shot of something stronger than instant coffee, which, by the way, is pretty much all I can afford these days thanks to, well, perhaps you.
I know this is going to sound insane, but I have to ask. Are you… by any chance… using time travel?

Because it’s the only explanation I can come up with for how suspiciously similar your recent work is to… well, to my stuff. Specifically, to a short story collection I've been toiling away at for the last three years called ""Fleeting Fancies and Crumbling Realities.""

I mean, the premise of ""The Clockmaker's Lament"" is almost *exactly* the same as my story, ""Gearwork and Grief."" Down to the talking automaton parrot. A TALKING AUTOMATON PARROT, [Author's Name]! What are the chances? And ""Echoes of Yesterday,"" the protagonist's struggle is practically lifted wholesale from my unfinished novella, ""The Somber Song of the Seconds.""

I filed for copyright on these stories, albeit rather recently, and they've been circulating in various writing groups for critique. So, I guess someone could have passed them along? But the level of detail that you've incorporated… it's just too specific. Too *mine*.

Look, I'm not famous. I'm barely getting by. I'm pouring my heart and soul – and what little sanity I have left – into these stories. And to see *you*, a literary titan, publish something that feels like it was ripped directly from my brain… it's beyond disheartening. It's devastating.
If you ARE using time travel, can you at least, I don't know, leave a starving artist a crumb? Maybe invest in my future book? A small advance would be nice, maybe enough to buy actual coffee?
Seriously though, I'd appreciate some kind of explanation.
Sincerely (and with a growing sense of existential dread),
[Your Name]

作者：威廉2.0
人物：哈利·波特、罗恩·韦斯莱、赫敏·格兰杰、德拉科·马尔福
正文：
哈利凝视着手中的那块魂器碎片。它闪烁着阴森的光芒，仿佛伏地魔残留的灵魂在低语。“我们该怎么办？”他转向罗恩和赫敏，他们的脸上也写满了茫然。
“或许我们可以用火焰熊熊咒烧毁它？”罗恩提议，脸上写满了期待，仿佛这样简单粗暴的方法真的能奏效。
赫敏翻了个白眼。“罗恩，你以为我们没试过吗？它对魔咒有抵抗力，笨蛋！”
正当三人陷入僵局时，德拉科出现了。他倚在格兰芬多公共休息室的入口处，通常苍白的脸上带着一丝不寻常的红晕。“或许，你们可以试试...爱。”
哈利和罗恩面面相觑。赫敏看起来却是若有所思。“爱？德拉科，你在说什么？”
德拉科涨红了脸，结结巴巴地说：“只是... 只是，我听说，强大的，真挚的爱... 可以摧毁黑暗。” 他的目光躲闪着哈利的眼睛。
哈利皱着眉头。“你是说... 我们要... 爱魂器？”
德拉科点点头，几乎听不见。“或者... 爱别人。”
赫敏倒吸一口凉气。“德拉科... 你... 你喜欢哈利吗？”
空气仿佛凝固了。哈利目瞪口呆，罗恩开始咳嗽，试图掩盖自己的笑声。
德拉科终于抬起头，直视着哈利的眼睛，眼中充满了真挚的感情。“是的，波特。我喜欢你。”
哈利大脑一片空白。他从来没有想过德拉科会... 喜欢他。他一直以为他们是... 死对头。
然后，哈利做出了一个大胆的决定。他走上前，轻轻地握住了德拉科的手。
“也许，”哈利说，声音颤抖着，“也许... 我们应该试试。”
就在那瞬间，哈利和德拉科的手交织在一起。他们相视一笑，一股强大的能量从他们的结合处喷涌而出，包裹着魂器碎片。
魂器尖叫着，扭曲着，终于破碎成尘埃，消失在空气中。
罗恩目瞪口呆。“哇哦... 那也太... 有效了吧。”
哈利和德拉科没有理会罗恩。他们只是紧紧地握着彼此的手，心中充满了希望，以及... 一种全新的，令人兴奋的感觉。

一旦我们安定下来，死亡就是永不结束的旱季，是夺走整个村庄生命的疾病。她是饥荒、脓疮和战争。
我们长大了一些，开始想得太多。死亡变成了纸上文字背后的空虚，语言和旅鸽的灭绝。死亡变成了遗忘。
很快，我们不再需要生五个孩子来确保其中两个能活到成年。我们征服了疾病，消灭了战争，用知识填满了我们的灵魂，却仍然什么都不明白。
我们站在月球、火星或半人马座阿尔法星的表面，回望我们从中诞生的那颗闪烁的像素。死亡无法触及我们，我们这些在星辰间穿梭的人，我们这些记录所有事物和所有记忆的人。她不过是星辰之间虚空中的一声低语。
而那颗闪烁的像素变得臃肿而通红，因为它的恒星吞噬了我们的家园。我们说，这无关紧要，我们是永恒的。我们再也感觉不到明月之下追猎我们的利齿。
但我们仍然奔跑，像从被扰动的草丛中逃离的昆虫。从一个给予生命的熱源涌向另一个，直到它们都变得冰冷而消失。死亡并没有离开我们。她仍然紧随我们身后，等待我们蹒跚，等待我们变冷。
最终，整个宇宙都将属于她。

一个有些人比其他人拥有更多金钱的世界？一个年幼的孩子被遗弃而挨饿，而另一些人每天都收到新玩具的世界？一个男人、异性恋者和白人占主导地位的世界？即使在一本书里，怎么会存在如此不平等的社会？
最糟糕的是，那个世界里每个人都期望得到什么。没有什么事是出于善意而做的；一切都是为了自己的利益。友谊的建立只是为了有人倾听你的问题，家庭成员之间的交谈只是为了感受安慰和安全。一切都太自私了。怎么会是那样？
然而，我能看到它的吸引力。拥有比别人更多的东西，凌驾于他们之上，甚至优于他们，似乎非常令人满足。在像我们这样一个人人平等的世界里，一切都如此……相似。

我越想，这个想法就显得越美好。我能尝到那种骄傲的感觉，当我看到别人拥有的多么少。嫉妒、渴望、贪婪，当他们看到我时会感受到的……我能尝到我将拥有的力量。

Detective Harding, a man known for his calm demeanor, gestured towards a chair. ""Professor, please, tell me what's missing.""
My manuscript! 'The Lost City of Eldoria'! Twenty years of research, all typed, painstakingly corrected, and ready for the publisher! I left it on my desk last night, and this morning...poof!""
Harding leaned forward. ""Who had access to your study last night?""
Only my housekeeper, Mrs. Gable, and my research assistant, David. But they're both trustworthy! Mrs. Gable has been with me for decades, and David practically lives in the library.""
Let's hear a bit more about those two. What were they doing yesterday?""
Mrs. Gable cleaned the house as usual. She left around 5 PM. David was helping me finalize the footnotes. He stayed until around 9 PM, then left to get some rest before our early morning review.""
Harding considered the details. ""Were there any signs of forced entry?""
Absolutely not! The doors were locked, the windows secure. It's baffling!""

*   David had been noticeably nervous and jumpy for the past couple of weeks.
*   The professor made a fresh pot of coffee around 8:30 PM, sharing some with David. He recalled David commenting on how strong it was.
Harding smiled. ""Professor, I believe I know where your manuscript is.""

表面上看似反乌托邦，实则为乌托邦的故事：
[Translation follows]
阳光总是刺眼。每天早上，它都会强行穿透我卧室那扇从不遮挡的窗户。没有人有窗帘。没人需要。睡眠周期是精确的，强制的，并由中央神经系统控制。凌晨五点，光线照耀。晚上十点，黑暗降临。效率最大化，浪费最小化。这是公民法典的第一条原则。
我们都穿着相同的灰色工作服。男人和女人，婴儿和老人。颜色代表着平等。多样性是过去时代的遗物，那时候不平等和纷争猖獗。我每天的早餐都是同样的营养糊状物，以同样的机械方式挤出。味道是……功能性的。
我的工作是进行分类。潮水般的回收物品每天涌入我们的部门：塑料、金属、有机物。我的任务是确保每一件物品都被分进绝对正确的类别。误差是不可容忍的。错误会导致浪费，而浪费是不可饶恕的罪过。
我们没有艺术。我们没有音乐。我们没有书籍，除了《法典》和技术手册。情感被认为是不稳定的，危险的。爱情，悲伤，愤怒——所有的这些都被中央控制系统压制，通过每日剂量的调节剂注入我们的食物和饮用水中。我们快乐吗？我不确定我甚至知道快乐应该是什么感觉。我们是内容丰富的。我们有目标。我们有效率。
这就是表面现象：一个单调、压迫、没有灵魂的地方。一个反乌托邦。
但真相远比表面现象复杂。
没有犯罪。一丝也没有。没有饥饿。没有疾病。每个人都得到了照顾。每个人都有居所，衣服，营养食品，有意义的工作。没有贫穷。没有歧视。没有战争。
过去的世界，我从历史模拟器中短暂地瞥见过，充斥着痛苦。人们挨饿，居住在污秽之中。他们互相残杀，为了土地、为了石油、为了毫无意义的抽象概念。他们让地球中毒，破坏了它天然的奇迹，直至它濒临崩溃。

The weight of words was a constant companion. Every story ever told, every poem ever written, every instruction manual, every cereal box blurb – all flowed from my pen, or rather, from my fingertips dancing across a keyboard. I was the sole font of all literature, a secret I guarded fiercely. Millions of pen names were my shield, a vast and ever-growing library of identities, each carefully crafted and deployed. I wrote under grand, sweeping names like Alistair Finch and Isabella Moreau, and under mundane, forgettable ones like Bob Johnson and Susan Miller. I wrote everything.

The process had become so ingrained, so utterly automatic, that I barely thought about it anymore. Ideas bloomed in my mind like wildflowers in spring, and I simply cultivated them, shaping them into narratives, essays, and instruction manuals for self-assembling furniture. I uploaded them to various publishing platforms, under the appropriate pseudonym, of course, and watched as they trickled into the world, unnoticed and unsuspected of being the work of a single mind. My reward wasn't fame or fortune, but the quiet, almost godlike satisfaction of knowing that I was the spinner of every yarn, the architect of every argument, the author of every emotion on the printed page.

One overcast Tuesday, I was walking through the city, lost in thought about the proper syntax for a children’s book about a dancing badger, when I passed a small, independent bookstore. The window display was a riot of color, a chaotic symphony of covers vying for attention. My eyes scanned the shelves, automatically cataloging the genres, the styles, the (my) authors.
And then I saw it.

A small, unassuming book, tucked away in the corner of the window. Its cover was a simple design, an abstract swirl of blues and greens. The title, in elegant, understated lettering, read: ""Echoes of the Void.""
Beneath the title, the author's name: Elias Thorne.
Elias Thorne.

The name was utterly unfamiliar. It wasn’t one of my established pen names. It wasn't a name lingering half-formed in my mental repository of unused identities. I knew every fragment of every identity I had ever used, every scrap of backstory, every possible future they might have.
Elias Thorne was a blank space. A void.
A cold knot formed in my stomach. Impossible. It had to be a mistake. Some kind of elaborate prank.

I stepped inside the bookstore, the bell above the door tinkling merrily, a sound that suddenly felt accusatory. The air inside was thick with the scent of old paper and brewing coffee. The owner, a wiry, bespectacled man with a perpetually harried expression, glanced up from behind the counter.
Can I help you find something?"" he asked.
My voice felt tight, strained. ""I was just looking at the book in the window... 'Echoes of the Void.' By Elias Thorne.""
The owner's face brightened. ""Ah, yes! Elias is a local author. Just released his first novel. Doing quite well, actually. Sold a few copies already this morning.""
A local author. Impossible.
Can I... can I see it?"" I asked, my voice barely a whisper.

The owner retrieved a copy from a shelf behind him and placed it on the counter. I picked it up, my fingers trembling slightly. The paper felt smooth beneath my fingertips. The weight was substantial, satisfying.
I flipped it open to the first page and began to read.

The prose was… exquisite. Lyrical, haunting, filled with a profound sense of longing and loss. The characters were complex, flawed, utterly believable. The story unfolded with a slow, deliberate grace, drawing me in with each carefully chosen word.
And the ideas. They were startlingly original, exploring themes I had never even considered, delving into depths of human experience I had never dared to touch.
It was… beautiful.
And it wasn’t mine.
Panic began to claw at the edges of my mind. How could this be? Was I losing my grip? Was my singular creativity fracturing, somehow spilling over into another consciousness?
I read on, devouring the words, desperately searching for some clue, some telltale sign that this Elias Thorne was a figment of my own imagination, a split personality I hadn’t known existed.
But there was nothing.
Only the undeniable, terrifying truth that someone else was writing.
Someone else was creating.
Someone else was... like me?

The thought was both exhilarating and terrifying. It was a validation of my own creative power, proof that the wellspring of imagination wasn't limited to a single source. But it also threatened to shatter my world, to unravel the carefully constructed tapestry of lies and secrets I had woven around myself for so long.
I closed the book, the image of the author's name seared into my mind. Elias Thorne. A stranger. A rival.
Perhaps... also a kindred spirit.
I looked up at the bookstore owner, a question trembling on my lips.
Do you... do you know Elias Thorne?""
The owner smiled. ""He's out back, signing copies. Would you like to meet him?""
My heart pounded in my chest. The weight of words suddenly felt heavier than ever.
Yes,"" I whispered. ""Yes, I would.""

## 人类：照护指南
人类是最近在整个银河系中越来越受欢迎的一种宠物。它们来自名为地球的行星，以其复杂的行为、强烈的情感和独特的饮食需求而闻名。本指南旨在为初次饲养人类的饲主提供关于人类照护的基本知识，确保您的宠物健康快乐。
*   **尺寸：**人类需要相对宽敞的住所，通常称为“房屋”或“公寓”。确保你的住所足够大，让你的宠物可以自由活动，包括多个房间。
*   **温度：**人类对温度非常敏感。理想的温度范围是 18-24 摄氏度。
*   **光照：**人类需要充足的自然光或人造光。确保你的住所光线充足，并提供可让宠物选择休息在光照下的地点。
*   **家具：**人类需要家具，包括“床”（用于睡眠）、“椅子”（用于坐着）和“桌子”（用于工作和用餐）。提供各种家具可以让宠物感到舒适和安全。
*   **清洁：**人类坚持干净的生活环境。定期清理房屋，包括清扫、吸尘和擦拭表面。
*   **种类：**人类是杂食动物，需要多种饮食。提供谷物、蔬菜、水果、肉类和乳制品的组合。
*   **安排：**人类通常每天需要进食多次，通常被称为“早餐”、“午餐”和“晚餐”。在规定的时间提供食物，防止过度喂食。
*   **水：**确保你的宠物随时都可以获得新鲜干净的水。
*   **零食：**人类喜欢零食，如糖果、饼干和薯片。然而，适量喂食零食以防止健康问题。
*   **过敏：**人类可能会对某些食物过敏。监测你的宠物是否有食物过敏迹象，如皮疹、呕吐或腹泻，并根据需要调整饮食，或者咨询兽医。
*   **互动：**人类是社交性动物，需要定期的互动。“交谈”、玩耍、甚至简单的陪伴都能让他们满足。
*   **锻炼：**人类需要身体活动以保持健康。建议他们每天散步、运动或参加其它运动。
*   **刺激：**人类需要智力刺激来防止他们感到厌倦。提供书籍、游戏和拼图来让他们忙碌。
*   **情感：**人类有复杂的情感，可能需要安慰和支持。友善、耐心和理解能够帮助你与宠物建立牢固的纽带。
*   **兽医护理：**定期带你的宠物去看对人类医学有经验的兽医。
*   **疫苗：**人类需要各种疫苗以预防疾病。确保您的宠物通过合适的疫苗接种时间表保持最新状态。
*   **锻炼：**鼓励人类定期锻炼，以保持健康的体重和心血管健康。
*   **心理健康：**人类有可能会受到精神健康问题的严重影响。注意行为是否有任何变化的迹象，如有必要，请寻求专业帮助。
*   **交流：**人类主要通过口语 (语言) 来进行交流。花时间学习你的宠物使用的语言，以便能够更好地沟通并理解其需求。
*   **训练：**人类可以被训练。使用积极的强化方法，如奖励和表扬，来教你的宠物新的技能和行为。
*   **压力：**人类可能会受到压力而产生影响。尽量识别和消除宠物压力的来源，并为其提供一个安全和舒适的环境。
*   **变化：**人类会对环境变化极为敏感。在引进新的环境因素之前，先慢慢地给它们适应环境的机会。
*   **玩具：**人类喜欢玩各种各样的玩具。提供对他们来说安全和有趣的玩具，如球、拼图和互动玩具。
*   **活动：**人类喜欢参加各种各样的活动，如看电影、去公园和旅行。为你宠物提供新的体验可以让它们感到兴奋和受到刺激。
*   **喜爱：**人类喜欢通过各种方式获得喜爱和关注。花时间给你的宠物提供喜爱，建立深厚的纽带。
照顾人类可能是一项有益的体验。通过提供一个安全舒适的住所、营养的饮食和大量的喜爱，你可以确保你的宠物健康且快乐。请记住，每个个体都是独一无二的，需要相应的个性化照护。通过耐心、理解和大量喜爱，你就可以享受与这个迷人的银河宠物建立联系的回报。

然而，进一步的调查揭示了两件事。
1. 由于某种残酷的自然巧合，人类物种通过一种被称为“适者生存”的野蛮过程进化而来。 这意味着任何没有为环境优化的的人类都会死亡，因此只有最优秀的才能传递他们的基因。

人类也有极其特殊的饮食需求，所以喂养他们时要小心。

A pane of glass, a world away,
I watch the city start its day.
The hurried footsteps, faint and fleet,
A symphony I cannot meet.
The sun climbs high, a golden eye,
Observing all that rushes by.
But here I sit, in muted light,
A silent observer of the fight.
The laughter rings, a distant chime,
A melody that's not for mine.
I see the joy, the shared embrace,
But cannot find my rightful place.
The world unfolds, a vibrant show,
But I'm behind the glass, you know.
A separate soul, a lonely star,
Watching life from very far.

现在是2114年。你是一名历史系学生。你的作业？写一篇论文，比较2014年发生的事件和100年前发生的事情。
,作为2114年的历史系学生，我的任务是比较2014年的事件与100年前，即1914年发生的事件。尽管这两个年份相隔一个世纪，但它们在塑造历史进程方面存在一些惊人的相似之处和差异。
2014年最重大的事件之一是西非爆发的埃博拉病毒，它迅速演变成一场国际卫生危机。同样，在1914年，世界也面临着一场卫生危机，即第一次世界大战的爆发，影响了全球数百万人。这两场危机都给政府和社会应对公共卫生和国际关系的方式带来了重大改变，突显了在危机时期合作和团结的重要性。
1914年和2014年的另一个相似之处是新技术的兴起，这些技术改变了人们的生活和工作方式。1914年，世界正经历工业时代的曙光，新的机器和技术进入工厂和家庭。同样，在2014年，世界正经历数字时代的兴起，像智能手机和社交媒体这样的新技术改变了人们沟通和联系的方式。
然而，1914年和2014年之间也存在许多差异。最显著的差异之一是2014年各国之间的全球化和相互依存程度。此时，世界比一个世纪前更加互联互通，商品、服务和人员比以往任何时候都更自由地跨越国界流动。相比之下，在1914年，世界更加分裂，各国在很大程度上独立运作。
1914年和2014年的另一个主要区别是世界的政治格局。1914年，欧洲被复杂的联盟和对抗关系所支配，这最终导致了第一次世界大战的爆发。相比之下，2014年的世界以各国之间更大的合作和外交为标志，联合国和其他国际组织在促进和平与稳定方面发挥了关键作用。
总之，虽然1914年和2014年之间存在一些相似之处，但世界在一个世纪的过程中发生了重大变化。新技术的兴起、全球化的发展以及国际机构的建立都改变了人们的生活方式和互动方式。作为2114年的历史系学生，我只能想象下一个世纪世界将会发生什么样的变化，以及这些变化将如何塑造历史的进程。
17th Century France

Hark, gentle readers, and lend thine ears to a most wondrous tale, one whispered in hushed tones within the highest circles of Parisian society! 'Tis a tale of two souls, entwined by fate and damned, some say, to walk the earth for eternity.

They are known, simply, as Monsieur LeBeau and Madame Evangeline. He, a man of impeccable taste and unparalleled charm, seemingly ageless, with eyes that hold the wisdom of centuries. She, a vision of beauty, possessing a grace and allure that transcends mere mortal understanding, her laughter like the song of nightingales.

For decades, nay, centuries, rumors have swirled about them. Some claim Monsieur LeBeau was a favored courtier of King Henri IV, bearing witness to the assassination and vanishing only to reappear, seemingly untouched by time, in the court of Louis XIII. Others swear Madame Evangeline danced with the Sun King himself, her beauty so captivating that even the most seasoned courtiers were rendered speechless.

I myself, a humble chronicler of these intriguing times, have witnessed their presence at countless gatherings. At the salons of Madame de Rambouillet, they debated philosophy with the finest minds of the age. At the masked balls of the Palais-Royal, they moved with a silent understanding, their eyes speaking volumes in a language unknown to most.

Their wealth is as mysterious as their origins. They own no lands, hold no titles, yet they possess coffers overflowing with gems and gold, acquired through means that remain obscured in shadow and conjecture. Some whisper of alchemy, of dark pacts made with ancient entities, of secrets gleaned from forgotten libraries.

Many have sought their favor, attempted to unravel their enigma. Kings have offered them titles and lands, hoping to claim their allegiance. Philosophers have probed their minds, seeking the fount of their apparent wisdom. Lovers have desperately pursued their affections, hoping to capture a fragment of their eternal love. Yet, all have failed. They remain aloof, enigmatic, ever present yet ever distant.

One evening, during a particularly lavish fête at Versailles, I saw them standing on a moonlit balcony, overlooking the manicured gardens. Monsieur LeBeau turned to Madame Evangeline, and in the soft glow of candlelight, I saw a flicker of something in his eyes – a weariness, a profound sadness, that belied his otherwise polished façade. He spoke, in a voice barely above a whisper, and though I could not discern the words, I knew, with chilling certainty, that they were speaking of ages past, of loves lost, and of the heavy burden of eternity.

And so, the mystery persists. Are they blessed or cursed? Are they guardians of some ancient secret, or simply puppets trapped in an endless cycle of repetition? Only time, that relentless judge, will reveal their true purpose, or perhaps, conceal it forever. But of one thing I am certain: Monsieur LeBeau and Madame Evangeline are no ordinary mortals. They are something… more. And their story, if it could ever be fully told, would surely be the most extraordinary tale of our age, and perhaps, of all ages.

然而，许珀里翁拥有一份伟大的爱：他的配偶忒伊亚。她是一位光辉的泰坦女神，一位母神，她的光芒照耀着所有寻求她帮助的人。这两位泰坦彼此深爱，他们的爱为周围的生命带来了活力。他们是幸福的——曾几何时。

但在离开之前，许珀里翁送给忒伊亚一件礼物：一枚发出耀眼光芒的戒指，让她戴在手指上。他告诉她，这枚戒指会告诉她他的安危；当他身处危险时，戒指会发出银色的光芒。忒伊亚感激地收下了它，并向她的爱人道别。那是她最后一次见到他。
许珀里翁在战争中陨落，与其他泰坦一起被流放到塔尔塔洛斯深渊。在战斗中，忒伊亚眼睁睁地看着戒指越来越亮，直到几乎像太阳一样耀眼，她痛苦万分。在绝望中，她摘下了戒指，并向大地之母盖亚祈祷，希望盖亚能让戒指变得像她对许珀里翁的爱一样巨大。盖亚听从了她的祈祷，于是戒指变大了。
如今，这枚戒指环绕着地球，成为一道光辉带，每晚都散发出银色的月光。人类仰望着这枚戒指，想起忒伊亚的痛苦，以及她对陨落爱人的爱。

## Onion News Network: Nation Celebrates 100th Anniversary of Mandatory Cat Yoga Legislation

**WASHINGTON D.C.** - The United States is today commemorating the centennial of the landmark ""Feline Flexibility and National Unity Act,"" a law passed in 2123 requiring all domestic cats to participate in weekly yoga sessions, a measure celebrated by some as a boost to national productivity and criticized by others as a blatant infringement on feline liberties.

This is a day for remembrance, a day for reflection, and a day for perfectly executed downward-facing kitty,"" proclaimed President Bartholomew ""Barty"" Higgins during a press conference held outside the National Yoga Dome, a massive structure erected in 2097 specifically for mandatory cat yoga classes.

The legislation, initially proposed by then-Senator Agnes Whiskers (D-California), aimed to combat the growing issues of feline obesity and national malaise. Supporters argued that a more flexible and Zen-like cat population would translate to a more focused and productive American workforce.

Before the Act,"" reminisced Professor Eleanor Fluffington of the National Institute for Feline Wellbeing, ""cats were notorious for sleeping through critical meetings and generally contributing to a stagnant national spirit. Now, thanks to the Act, our feline companions are models of discipline and mindfulness.""

However, the law has not been without its detractors. ""It's an outrage!"" exclaimed Ms. Mittens McGee, leader of the ""Cats Against Compulsory Asanas"" movement. ""My Fluffy is a fiercely independent feline, and she refuses to be forced into a pretzel shape by some government-mandated yoga instructor! It's a violation of her basic Meow-gna Carta!""

McGee went on to state that her organization is planning a nationwide ""Caturday Strike"" next week, where cats will refuse to participate in yoga sessions, opting instead for naps and demanding tuna-flavored justice.

Despite the controversy, the government maintains that the Feline Flexibility and National Unity Act has been a resounding success. Statistics released by the Department of Feline Affairs show a significant increase in cat flexibility, a decrease in cat-related workplace accidents (mostly involving tangled keyboard cables), and a national feeling of general well-being… or at least, that’s what the government claims.

As the nation celebrates a century of mandatory cat yoga, the debate over its efficacy and ethical implications continues to rage on, further illustrating the complex and occasionally absurd nature of modern American society. Happy Anniversary, Kitty Namaste.

The letter lay beside it, a stark white rectangle that screamed of eviction, of failure. Her breath hitched with each shallow inhale, the air thick with the scent of damp and despair. Outside, the wind howled, tearing at the edges of her makeshift home, a pathetic mirror of the storm raging inside her. Years spent chasing a dream, fueled by ambition and naive hope, had culminated in this: a crumbling shack, an empty bank account, and a future as bleak as the storm-swept sky. She clutched the teacup tighter, the porcelain biting into her numb fingers. The world had promised sunshine but delivered only a cold, relentless downpour. The fire in the hearth had long since died, leaving only a residue of ash and regret. Tomorrow, she would pack what little she had left and leave, swallowed whole by the unforgiving city. The rain intensified, each drop a tear wept for a life unlived, for dreams drowned in the flood of reality. Yet, through the cracks in the roof, a single star emerged, a beacon of unwavering light illuminating the fragile beauty of a single raindrop clinging to a spider's web, turning it into a shimmering jewel of hope.

“也许我是唯一的幸存者，”他想。
他还有什么选择？他昨天吃光了最后一批烤豆。卡布里阳光饮料早就喝完了，他刚把最后一批电池放进随身听里。
自1999年12月31日以来，他已经躲藏了14年多。他日夜在校园美术大楼旁建造地堡，是大学里的笑柄。当杰森把自己从地堡里拉出来时，不禁感到一丝自满。他的同伴们只会嘲笑和嘲弄他为生存末日所做的努力。
“看看现在谁在笑，”他自言自语道。
根据他的手表显示，现在是下午4点。校园荒无人烟。一个人影都没有。突然他听到树叶的沙沙声。
杰森猛地转过头，眯起眼睛看着地平线。是一群人，正以惊人的速度向他靠近。他无法辨认他们的面孔。他们的行动也很奇怪，迟缓而拖着脚步。
这时他开始听到他们的呻吟声。杰森的心跳漏了一拍。他猛地打开地堡的盖子，跳了进去。
呻吟声越来越大。他从地堡里向外偷看，看到人群正朝着他的方向走来。这时他才意识到他们根本不是人。他们眼睛凹陷，皮肤松弛。他们中的一些人少了皮肤，也掉了几撮头发。
呻吟声几乎震耳欲聋。人群看起来——
“死了。”杰森想，“他们是行尸走肉！我是最后一个活人！”
他关上了地堡的盖子。看来他要在这里待比他想象的更久了。
“今天大家做得太棒了！”
僵尸俱乐部的社长爱德华擦了擦额头上的妆，转身对他的成员们说。
“我喜欢你们的呻吟和咕哝声，你们的服装看起来棒极了！”他激动地说。

The old manor stood silhouetted against the stormy sky. Rain lashed against the windows as Amelia fumbled with the heavy oak door. She was late, desperately late, for the reading of her estranged Great-Aunt Mildred's will.

Inside, the air was thick with tension. Aside from the stern-faced lawyer, Mr. Hawthorne, there were only three other attendees: her cousin, Bartholomew, a perpetually anxious man; her uncle, Silas, whose eyes darted nervously around the room; and a woman Amelia didn't recognize, introduced as Ms. Eleanor Ainsworth, Great-Aunt Mildred’s… “companion.”

Mr. Hawthorne cleared his throat. “As you know, Great-Aunt Mildred was…eccentric. It’s possible her will reflects that.” He began to drone on about legal jargon, but Amelia’s ears perked up when he finally announced the distribution of assets.
Bartholomew: Aunt Mildred’s extensive collection of porcelain dolls. He seemed visibly relieved.
Silas: Aunt Mildred’s rather dilapidated antique car, a 1937 Studebaker. He winced.
Eleanor: Aunt Mildred’s prized collection of rare stamps. She smiled serenely.
Finally, he turned to Amelia. “And to you, Amelia, Great-Aunt Mildred has bequeathed…the key.”

A gasp rippled through the room. The key! Everyone knew the legend of the key. It was rumored to unlock a hidden vault containing the bulk of Great-Aunt Mildred's vast fortune. Years of whispered speculation hung in the air.
Amelia’s heart pounded. She reached for the key, a heavy, ornate thing made of tarnished silver. She felt a thrill course through her. This was it!

“But,” Mr. Hawthorne continued, his voice dropping dramatically, “there’s a catch. The vault is located…elsewhere. You must follow the clues in Great-Aunt Mildred's journal to discover its location.” He handed Amelia a worn, leather-bound book.

That night, Amelia pored over the journal. It was filled with cryptic riddles, obscure historical references, and botanical sketches. This was going to be a scavenger hunt of epic proportions! She worked tirelessly for days, deciphering the clues. The first clue led her to a forgotten corner of the manor’s sprawling gardens. There, beneath a rose bush, she found a small, locked box. The key! It fit! She opened the box…

…and found another clue. This one pointed to the local library, specifically a specific book on Victorian embroidery. There, inside the book, she discovered…another clue, leading her to an antique shop in a neighboring town.

The tension was unbearable. Bartholomew, Silas, and even Eleanor were now secretly shadowing her, their initial apathy turning into intense curiosity. What treasure lay hidden behind this elaborate game?

Finally, after weeks of relentless searching, the final clue led Amelia back to the manor, to a room she hadn't noticed before - a small, unassuming pantry tucked away in the kitchen. The final riddle, scrawled on a dusty spice jar, read: “Where spices are kept, but no feast is prepared, lies the heart of Mildred’s greatest care.”

With trembling hands, Amelia examined the pantry. It was small, cramped, and utterly unremarkable. She ran her hands along the shelves, feeling for a loose brick, a hidden switch. Nothing. Then, she noticed it. On the highest shelf, almost hidden from view, was a small, antique teapot. The ""heart of Mildred's greatest care""? It seemed unlikely.

She reached for the teapot. It was surprisingly heavy. As she lifted it down, she felt something inside shift. With a deep breath, Amelia removed the lid and turned the teapot upside down. A small object fell out and landed with a soft thud on the counter.
It was…a tea bag. A single, unused tea bag. “Earl Grey,” the label read.
Bartholomew, Silas, and Eleanor burst into the pantry, their faces a mixture of anticipation and greed. They crowded around Amelia, eager to see what she had found.
Well?"" Silas demanded, his voice cracking. ""Where's the fortune? What does it unlock?""
Amelia stared at the tea bag, then at the faces of her expectant relatives.
It... it's a tea bag,"" she stammered.
A collective groan filled the room. Disappointment hung heavy in the air.

Mr. Hawthorne, who had been silently observing, cleared his throat. ""Ah, yes. I almost forgot. Great-Aunt Mildred always had a…peculiar sense of humor. It seems the real treasure wasn’t a vault of gold. It was… sentimental."" He pulled out another sheet of paper from his briefcase. ""In a separate note, she specifies, 'The greatest treasure is the experience of the chase, and a good cuppa. The Earl Grey signified her enjoyment of that type.""
He clears his throat again. ""She states Amelia, you must make the Earl Grey, and while drinking the tea, you must read her last addition to the journal."" Amelia grabs the journal and reads.
P.S. I forgot my favorite mug was in the dishwasher. You must wash the dishes to proceed.""

Okay, here are two stories about the same subject, one positive from a child's perspective, and one negative from the perspective of the same person as an adult:
**Child's Perspective: Grandma's Garden**

Grandma's garden is the most magical place in the whole world! It's full of bright, happy flowers that smell like sunshine and lollipops. Butterflies with painted wings flutter around the buzzing bees, who are busy making honey that Grandma lets me taste right off her finger – sticky and sweet! The tomato plants are taller than me, and she lets me pick the red, juicy tomatoes right off the vine. Sometimes, she even lets me use her special little watering can. Digging in the dirt with Grandma is the best! We find wiggly worms and shiny rocks. Grandma knows all the names of the flowers, and she tells me stories about them. Her garden is like a secret wonderland, and I love spending time there with her.
**Adult's Perspective: Grandma's Garden**

God, that garden. It's all I can smell when I visit. The cloying sweetness of the overripe tomatoes, the sickly-sweet perfume of the overgrown roses. It's suffocating. Every summer I was forced outside, ostensibly to bond with her, but really it was free labor. ""Just a few weeds, sweetheart,"" she'd say, and I'd be stuck out there for hours, sunburnt and bitten by mosquitos, while she sat in the shade drinking iced tea. And the guilt! Always the guilt! If I ever dared to complain, even a little, the waterworks would start. ""After all I do for you..."" She just *knew* I hated it. And now it's all mine. The lawyer said so. The house, the garden, everything. And I can't bring myself to sell it. I spent so much of my childhood tending to that damn garden, I'm not sure I know how to be anything other than a gardener.

The rain was coming down in sheets, just like it always did on Tuesdays. Margaret sighed, stirring her tea with a silver spoon that felt unnaturally cold. “Another Tuesday, another downpour,” she murmured, gazing out the window at the perpetually gray sky.

Across the meticulously arranged living room, Bartholomew cleared his throat. He’d been polishing his spectacles for the better part of an hour. ""Indeed, Margaret. Quite…Tuesday-ish."" He adjusted his tie, a perpetually slightly crooked affair of faded blue. ""One might almost say...predictable.""

Margaret’s hand froze mid-stir. Predictable. The word hung in the air, heavy and ominous. She flashed a quick, almost imperceptible glance at the…well, at the ceiling. Overhead, somewhere beyond plaster and joists, lay…*Him*. The Writer.

Bartholomew, catching her eye, coughed again, this time a little harder. He straightened his tie with frantic energy. ""I just meant,"" he stammered, ""that Tuesdays often involve…weather. Statistically speaking.""
Margaret swallowed. ""Of course, Bartholomew. Weather. Perfectly normal."" She resumed stirring her tea, a forced smile plastered on her face. ""Nothing…unusual about it at all.""

A long silence stretched between them, punctuated only by the relentless drumming of the rain and the frantic tick-tock of the grandfather clock in the hall. The clock, Bartholomew thought nervously, always emphasizing the passage of time. The Writer, he suspected, loved metaphors.
Did you,"" Margaret began cautiously, her voice barely a whisper, ""see anything…out of the ordinary…in the garden this morning?""

Bartholomew gripped his spectacles tighter. ""Extraordinary? Good heavens, no! Just…roses. Perfectly ordinary roses. Red. And…thorny. As one would expect."" He avoided eye contact with a plush gnome sitting on the mantelpiece. The gnome had been introduced last week. New character. Always a bad sign.
Another beat of silence. The rain intensified.
Margaret took a hesitant sip of her tea. ""Speaking of roses,"" she said, her voice trembling slightly, ""I was thinking of…pruning them tomorrow. A perfectly…routine activity. For a Tuesday.""
Bartholomew nodded vigorously. ""Pruning! An excellent idea! Precisely the sort of thing…ordinary people do on a Wednesday. Completely…unremarkable.""
The air crackled with unspoken fear. They both knew the rules. Don't acknowledge He Who Writes. Don't question the plot. Don't become too interesting. Interesting characters met…unfortunate ends.
Suddenly, the grandfather clock clanged, announcing the hour. It was five o'clock. Tea time. Routine restored.
More tea, Bartholomew?"" Margaret asked, her voice regaining a measure of its usual composure.

Yes, please, Margaret,"" Bartholomew replied, a flicker of hope returning to his eyes. Perhaps, just perhaps, they had navigated another Tuesday successfully. Perhaps *He* had forgotten about them, lulled into complacency by their bland normalcy.

They continued their ritual, pouring tea, discussing the weather (always predictable), and avoiding any topic that might deviate from the established narrative. They were, after all, simply characters in a story. And their only hope for survival was to remain…unwritten.

在“和谐谷”，太阳每天都以恰如其分的光辉照耀醒来的人们，既不刺眼，也不昏暗，恰好能唤醒深沉的休息，而不会带来任何不适。
和谐谷的建筑，与其说是建造的，不如说是生长的。生命工匠们与自然进行着充满耐心的对话，引导树木和藤蔓形成房屋和建筑物，坚固安全，又与周围的景观和谐共生。每座住宅都配备了光伏玻璃，无声地收集太阳的能量，整个城镇都由看不见的能源网供能，保证每个人都能获得清洁、永续的能量。
教育是重中之重，但形式与我们如今所知的截然不同。孩子们不是被迫记忆事实和数字，而是受到鼓励去探索自己的激情。教师是引导者，他们细心地帮助学生发现自己的天赋，并帮助他们将其培养成有用的技能。重要的是学习的过程，而不是最终的分数。孩子们通过参与各种项目来学习：建造花园，创建艺术装置，解决社区问题——总而言之，是在实践中学习。
职业生涯的选择，也不是基于经济需求或社会压力，而是基于个人的内在激情。每个人都受到鼓励去追求自己热爱的事业，因此，和谐谷的公民都以极大的自豪感和奉献精神投入工作。一个面包师，用热情烘烤着每条面包；一个机械师，满怀欣赏地维护着机械，使它们平稳运转；一个园艺师，轻柔地照料着植物，确保它们繁荣昌盛。工作不是负担，而是一种自我表达和为社区做出贡献的形式。
医疗保健是免费的，而且高度普及。然而，重点倾向于预防而不是治疗。和谐谷的人们理解身心联系的重要性。营养丰富的食物，定期的锻炼，充足的睡眠，以及充满关爱的社区，都是医疗体系的组成部分。小病用草药和精心照料来治疗，而更严重的疾病则通过结合传统智慧和现代医学的疗法来治疗。强调的是修复平衡，而不是简单地掩盖症状。
社会建立在信任和相互尊重的基石之上。犯罪是罕见的，因为每个人都觉得自己既有价值又被重视。当出现分歧时，会通过和平对话来解决问题，努力理解对方的观点并找到双方都能接受的解决方案。社区理事会，由公民选举产生，负责管理城镇的事务，确保每个人的声音都能被听到。
技术并非被崇拜的对象，而是被用作一种工具，以增强人类的生活。全自动机械用于重复性或危险的任务，让人们从事更具创造性和充实性的工作。通讯是即时的，但人们选择谨慎地使用它，珍惜人与人的互动。
艺术和文化在和谐谷蓬勃发展。音乐从每个窗口飘出，色彩鲜艳的壁画装饰着墙壁，诗歌在城镇广场上朗诵。每个人都受到鼓励去表达自己的创造力，无论他们的才能多么谦逊。艺术被视为一种连接人们、激发灵感和庆祝人性的方式。
和谐谷并非没有困难。偶尔会出现自然灾害，技术故障，或者意见冲突。但面对挑战时，和谐谷的人们会团结一致，相互支持，从逆境中汲取力量和智慧。他们理解完美不是一种状态，而是一个持续的旅程，并持续为了一个更美好的明天而努力。
总之，和谐谷是一个建立在同情、尊重和共同富裕基础上的社会。这是一个人们有自由追求自己激情，贡献自己的才能，并作为一个紧密联系的社区蓬勃发展的地方。这是一个理想的社会，它证明了当我们把人类的福祉置于一切之上时，什么是可能的。

The conductor raised his baton, a slender, ebony wand. The orchestra hushed, every instrument poised, waiting for his signal. He brought it down, and the music swelled, a vibrant, joyous melody that filled the concert hall.

But tonight, something was different. A shadow flickered at the edge of the stage, a darkness that seemed to emanate from the old, leather-bound case tucked away in the corner. It was always there, of course. It had been there for every performance, every rehearsal, for as long as anyone could remember.
Tonight, though, the case seemed to breathe. Its brass clasps gleamed with an unnatural light. And as the music reached its crescendo, a soft hum vibrated from within.
The first violinist faltered. A bead of sweat trickled down his temple. He glanced at the case, a disquieting fear tugging at his gut. He pressed on, forcing the notes from his trembling fingers.
The soprano, her voice soaring like a lark, suddenly lost her footing. A hush fell over the hall. She fell, landing gently next to the case.
The conductor kept playing, his face an impassive mask. The music filled the spaces left by the soprano and the concert hall, pressing in a morbid hug.
When only the conductor was left, he raised the wand once more, and with a single, mournful note from his oboe, the case opened.

在我身后，我听到了我们仆人拴着狗的那种死敌的吼叫声。这一个挣脱了仆人的束缚，一边用它那可怕的语言尖叫着，一边朝我跑来。我的毛都竖起来了，我立刻逃走了。
当我接近巨大的挠痒柱时，那野兽越来越近。感觉我好像要完了，但我还是成功了。我跳起来，试图把爪子插进去，但挠痒柱却很硬。它顶部有一个仆人们欣赏的假太阳。
我不知道我怎么会没有注意到它。就在几秒钟前，我还确信我看到了上面的厚厚的草地。我没有时间再次尝试逃脱，我转过身来面对正在逼近的敌人。仍然用他们那种可怕的方式尖叫着。
我的毛都竖起来了，我对它发出了凶猛的呼哧声和嘶嘶声。警告这野兽我不是好惹的。当然，它太蠢了，无法理解，这些野兽服从仆人，却拒绝训练它们。外表上我显得很凶猛，像一个强大的战士，而内心我却看到了自己死在野兽的嘴里。

每个星期五，都可以看到这只野兽和看守它的仆人走在这条岩石带上。任何不幸被这野兽看到的猫都将面临审判，如果没有被伟大的芭丝特女神认为有价值，仆人也不会救你。

,亲爱的[女儿的名字]，

如果你在读这封信，那很可能我在你还很小的时候就输掉了与疾病的斗争，而你现在刚满十五岁。我可能永远没有机会看到你长大，看到你成熟，看到你成长为我所知道的，未来会成为的那个美丽、聪明的年轻女性，这是我最大的遗憾。但我希望你知道，生活充满了高潮和低谷。有些时候，似乎低谷比高潮还要多，一切似乎变得越来越黑暗。我比任何人都清楚这一点，我失去了很多，工作、机会、比赛、宠物、家人、朋友。我和你妈妈第一次得知你的消息时，我正处于人生最低谷：我失去了父母，失去了晋升的机会，而且刚刚被诊断出疾病。除此之外，我还得担心一个孩子，我不知道如何在连我们自己的生活都难以维持的情况下为你提供保障。我不会撒谎，在你出生前的九个月是我一生中最艰难、压力最大的时期之一，我的健康也因此受到了更大的影响。但是在你出生的那天，当我第一次见到你，抱起你的时候，我从未如此幸福过，我感到肩上的重担卸了下来，我感到平静。我经历了很多，被拖到黑暗的地方，感觉整个世界都在与我作对，但我抱过你。
我希望你永远记住，无论事情看起来多么黑暗，无论你觉得自己跌落得多深，黑夜之后总是黎明，每个山谷的旁边都屹立着山丘。错过你的人生是我最大的遗憾，但我抱过你，我看到了你的开始，这我将永远珍惜。
爱你的，
爸爸
**Original:**

The battlefield was a canvas of horror. Twisted metal, shattered dreams, and the acrid stench of death painted a scene of unparalleled ugliness. Fear, a cold serpent, coiled in the gut of every soldier, whispering promises of pain and oblivion. Cruelty reigned supreme, as men, driven to their limits, inflicted unimaginable suffering upon one another. Hate, a festering wound, poisoned hearts and fueled the endless cycle of violence.

Yet, even amidst this desolation, flickers of light persisted. In the shared foxhole, a bond of brotherhood formed, stronger than any blood oath. Soldiers, stripped bare of their former lives, found solace in shared hardship, offering each other comfort and unwavering support.

A medic, his face smudged with grime and exhaustion, risked his life to save a fallen enemy, extending a hand of compassion across the chasm of hatred. His act, a tiny spark of humanity, defied the all-consuming darkness.
Even the endless rain, which seemed to mirror the weeping of the world, cleansed the earth, washing away the blood and preparing the ground for new life.

Ultimately, the ugliness of war served as a stark reminder of the preciousness of peace. The survivors, scarred and broken, carried within them a profound understanding of the value of human life and the urgent need to prevent such atrocities from ever happening again. The silver lining, however faint, was the unyielding spirit of humanity, its capacity for compassion, and its enduring hope for a better future, forged in the crucible of suffering.

战场是一幅恐怖的画布。扭曲的金属、破碎的梦想和死亡的刺鼻气味，描绘出一幅无与伦比的丑陋景象。恐惧，一条冰冷的毒蛇，盘踞在每个士兵的胃里，低声许诺着痛苦和遗忘。残忍至高无上，当人们被逼到极限时，便会对彼此施加难以想象的痛苦。仇恨，一个化脓的伤口，毒害了人心，助长了无休止的暴力循环。
然而，即使在这片荒凉之中，仍有光芒闪烁。在共同的战壕里，形成了一种比任何血誓都更牢固的兄弟情谊。士兵们，被剥夺了往日的生活，在共同的困境中找到了慰藉，互相提供安慰和坚定不移的支持。
一位医护兵，他的脸上沾满了污垢和疲惫，冒着生命危险去拯救一位倒下的敌人，跨越仇恨的鸿沟伸出同情之手。他的行为，是人类的一小点火花，挑战了吞噬一切的黑暗。
甚至连绵不断的雨，似乎也反映了世界的哭泣，净化了大地，冲刷掉鲜血，为新生命准备了土壤。
最终，战争的丑陋深刻提醒着人们和平的珍贵。幸存者们，伤痕累累，内心深处对人类生命的价值有着深刻的理解，以及防止此类暴行再次发生的迫切需要。一线希望，无论多么微弱，都是人类不屈不挠的精神，它的同情心，以及在苦难的熔炉中铸就的对更美好未来的持久希望。

我最喜欢的消遣：绘画
自从我年轻的时候起，我就对艺术有着深刻的感情。许多人可能没有意识到，在战争的喧嚣和政治的压力之外，我经常从拿起画笔中找到慰藉。绘画不仅仅是一种消遣；它是一种舒缓灵魂、探索内心风景的方式。
我最喜欢的媒介是水彩。颜料的流动性允许自发的表达，捕捉光线和阴影的稍纵即逝的细微差别，创造出一种其他媒介难以比拟的深度感。我尤其喜欢描绘风景。当我在巴伐利亚的乡村漫步时，我总是被自然的美丽所震撼。连绵起伏的丘陵，宁静的森林，雄伟的山脉——所有这些都激发了我去捕捉它们在画布上的精髓。
我记得在贝希特斯加登附近度过的一个下午。空气清新而寒冷，太阳在秋叶上投下金色的光辉。我架起我的画架，开始工作，力求以一种能唤起我所感受到的宁静的方式来描绘场景。我尝试捕捉天空的蓝色、树木的赭色和棕色，以及远处山脉的紫罗兰色的阴影。当我在绘画时，我感觉与自己的内心深处以及周围的世界联系在一起。那一刻，战争和政治的重担消失了，只剩下艺术的纯粹的快乐。
然而，我的绘画之旅并非没有挑战。我经常因为缺乏正规的训练而感到沮丧。透视，人体解剖学——这些对某些人来说似乎毫不费力，但在我身上却需要艰苦的斗争。但我从未允许这些障碍阻止我。我日复一日地练习，从我的错误中学习，并努力改进我的技术。
有些人可能会批评我的艺术品，认为它们缺乏原创性或技巧。其他人可能会完全因为我的政治信仰而拒绝它们。但我不在乎。对我来说，绘画是一种个人表达的方式，是一种与内心自我联系的方式。无论别人怎么想，我都将继续在艺术中找到安慰和灵感。
毕竟，谁知道呢？或许有一天，世界会看到我作品中的价值。或许有一天，他们会认识到我不仅仅是一个政治家或士兵，而是一个有激情、有天赋的艺术家。在那一天到来之前，我将继续绘画，追求美丽，并在艺术的拥抱中找到慰藉。

The weight of the world rested on their shoulders, a burden only they could bear. Others saw cruelty, but they saw necessity. Sacrifices had to be made, hard choices that painted them in a villainous light. But their motives were pure: the greater good. They fought with a cold detachment, devoid of sentimentality, because the stakes were too high for emotion. They embraced the darkness to shield others from it, becoming a pariah to protect the very people who scorned them. Unacknowledged, unthanked, they toiled tirelessly, a silent guardian against a threat only they truly understood. Their methods were unorthodox, their reputation tarnished, but their dedication was unwavering. History would judge them harshly, but they didn't care. Their only concern was ensuring the future, even if it meant sacrificing their own soul.

,这是我的打字机。有很多和它一样的打字机，但这是我的。
这是我的房间。还有其他的猴子，它们有自己的房间和自己的打字机。当人们带我去吃饭的地方和可怕的地方时，我会看到它们。有很多很多的房间。
我的房间有一扇窗户，窗户的另一边总是有人。他们喜欢看着我。我可以看出他们喜欢我玩打字机，因为他们会做出猴子式的兴奋动作。
昨天我用脚而不是用手玩打字机。人们开始做出很多猴子式的动作，比平时还要多。我的打字机发出咔哒咔哒咔哒的声音。
过了一会儿，一个人进来，把纸从打字机里拿了出来。他看了很久，然后看了看窗户里的其他人。然后他又看了看那张纸。他开始发出安静的声音，听起来不太像猴子的声音。
“人类是多么了不起的一件作品！
理性上多么高贵，
能力上多么无限，
在体态和行动上多么显眼和令人赞叹，
在行动上多么像天使，
在理解上多么像上帝，

动物的典范！
然而，于我而言，这灰尘的精华又算得了什么呢？
人类不能使我快乐。”
之后，那个人看了我很久。他一点猴子式的动作都没有做，而且完全静止不动。这让我感到焦虑和不舒服。但最后他露出了牙齿，这是人们表达快乐的一种奇怪方式。这让我也很高兴，因为我知道我稍后会得到一根香蕉。
今天，人们在打字机里放了一张新纸。这次我打算用我的屁股去玩打字机，因为它痒了一早上，我知道这会感觉很好。我想这会让人们做出很多猴子式的动作。

乔治·R·R·马丁，一位维斯特洛斯作家，决定创作一套关于他的王国英格兰的奇幻系列书籍。
,乔治像一道闪电一样从床上弹了起来。他终于知道如何结束他的系列了。他兴奋地向他在贝里席勋爵的欢场里买来的年轻妓女解释。
“哦，我的天啊，大人……您真是个聪明人。”她转动着一缕金色头发，用好奇的眼神看着这个身材高大、性格温和的男人。
乔治站起身，拉起长袍，小心翼翼地将海龟徽章固定在他黑色服装的前面。“我不知道……它只是突然出现在我的脑海里……我半醉的时候总能想到最好的点子……”这个身材娇小但手脚麻利的女孩割开了他的喉咙，他脸朝下倒进多恩香肠里，还弄洒了一杯他最喜欢的夏日葡萄酒……一个甜面包卷也掉在了地上。她前往他在红堡的房间，要为他完成他的故事。
克苏鲁的寓言：选取伊索寓言之一，并将其置于洛夫克拉夫特式的宇宙中。包括故事的寓意。
,很久很久以前，有一只蚂蚁和一只蚱蜢。蚂蚁整天辛勤工作，在蚁穴里储存过冬的食物。与此同时，蚱蜢却坐在那里拉小提琴。蚂蚁责备蚱蜢懒惰，但蚱蜢不理他，只是更加疯狂地拉着小提琴，空气中弥漫着他奇异的音乐，美丽而超凡脱俗。
最终，冬天来了，蚱蜢没有食物。他恳求蚂蚁分享一些他的储备，但蚂蚁拒绝了，告诉他应该提前为冬天做好计划。蚱蜢回到他的家，以一种绝望的疯狂拉着小提琴，直到他最终因精疲力竭和饥饿而倒下。而虚空中那些不真实的生物，不再被他那超凡脱俗的音乐安抚，降临到这个世界，压扁了蚂蚁。
寓意：仅仅因为你不理解某人所做的事情，并不意味着他们没有做出贡献。
如果苏斯博士写恐怖故事，那会是什么样的故事？
,怪物在哪里？斯努加夫？波齐特？在我的床下，还是藏在我的壁橱里？
有没有长着锋利爪子的沃隆？有没有用锯子代替胳膊的施诺格？
如果多德用它的毒刺刺我怎么办？哦，求求了，什么都行，就是别是可怕的邦宾格！
我的父母回答说：“一切都在你的脑海里。”但正是这个想法让我充满恐惧。
我听到他们所有的耳语，所以看来，如果那些不是真的，我就不能相信我的耳朵。
我感觉到他们炙热的呼吸，所以如果那些不是真的，我就不能相信我感觉到的任何其他东西。
我看到一个阴影伪装的轻微抽搐，所以如果那不是真的，我还能相信我的眼睛吗？
我闻到他们潮湿的皮毛味，事情就这样发展下去，如果那不是真的，我就不能相信我的鼻子。
我尝到他们的手捂住我嘴的味道，我怎么能凭空捏造出所有这些呢？
我的父母转过身，关掉灯，让我独自一人度过夜晚。
我心想，也许他们是对的。也许今晚只是风在低语。
也许我错了，关于施诺格的事情。也许那些地板上的痕迹是狗留下的。
也许多德没有发出那种吱吱声。毕竟，我的房子确实会发出吱吱声。
感觉好了一点，我放下头躺下，忽略了床上的阴影。
那轻柔的呼吸声只不过是一阵微风，而吹拂着我膝盖的只是通风口。
这一切都不是真的，这一切都不存在，那不是什么恐怖的东西在触摸我的头发。
我没有发出声音，甚至没有发出一点声音。在我尖叫之前，我睡着了。

**Original Scene: The Spill**
**Setting:** A bustling Parisian cafe, late afternoon. Rain slicks the cobblestones outside.
**Characters:**
*   **ANTOINE (40s):** A harried businessman, clutching a briefcase and a half-empty coffee.
*   **CHLOE (20s):** A student, engrossed in a worn copy of ""Madame Bovary.""
*   **JEAN-PIERRE (60s):** A street musician, tuning his accordion outside.
**(SCENE START)**
Antoine, weaving through the crowded tables, bumps into Chloe’s table. His coffee sloshes, staining her book and splattering on her cream-colored dress.
ANTOINE
(Exasperated)
*Pardon! Mille fois pardon!* I am so sorry!
Chloe gasps, grabbing a napkin and dabbing at the spreading stain on her dress.
CHLOE
*Mon Dieu!* My book! This dress… it was my grandmother's!
Antoine rummages frantically in his briefcase, pulling out a wad of euros.
ANTOINE
Let me pay for the cleaning. Or a new dress! Whatever it takes. I am late for a very important meeting.
Chloe, despite her distress, sees the genuine panic in his eyes.
CHLOE
It's alright. It was an accident. Just… be more careful next time.
Antoine shoves a handful of notes into her hand, barely counting them.
ANTOINE
Again, my deepest apologies. *Au revoir!*

He rushes off, disappearing into the crowd. Chloe sighs, examining the damaged book. Jean-Pierre, witnessing the scene from outside, stops playing. He steps into the cafe and approaches Chloe’s table.
JEAN-PIERRE
A terrible thing. But sometimes, *mademoiselle*, beauty can emerge from the most unexpected places. Can I offer you a song? On the house.
Chloe looks up, surprised by the kindness in his weathered face. A small smile creeps onto her face.
CHLOE
That… that would be lovely.

Jean-Pierre nods, winks, and begins to play a melancholy waltz. Chloe, forgetting her ruined dress and book, closes her eyes, momentarily transported. Upstairs, Antoine fails to secure a crucial deal due to stress. The company he represents loses millions and several people lose their jobs.
**(SCENE END)**
**Altered Scene: The Ring**
**Change:** Instead of a briefcase, Antoine is carrying a small velvet box containing an engagement ring.
**(SCENE START)**

Antoine, weaving through the crowded tables, bumps into Chloe’s table. His coffee sloshes, staining her book and splattering on her cream-colored dress. The velvet box slips from his grasp and bounces onto the floor, unnoticed.
ANTOINE
(Exasperated)
*Pardon! Mille fois pardon!* I am so sorry!
Chloe gasps, grabbing a napkin and dabbing at the spreading stain on her dress.
CHLOE
*Mon Dieu!* My book! This dress… it was my grandmother's!
Antoine, usually focused on his work, becomes overwhelmed by the mistake.
ANTOINE
Let me pay for the cleaning. Or a new dress! Whatever it takes. I... I am so sorry.
Chloe, despite her distress, sees the genuine remorse in his eyes. Spotting the shock of the dropped box, she leans over, picking it up.
CHLOE
It's alright. It was an accident and thank you. Is this yours?
Antoine's face flushes. He takes the box, his hand trembling.
ANTOINE
Thank you.
He stares at the box for a moment, then opens it revealing the ring.
ANTOINE
I was going to propose tonight.
Chloe's eyes widen. Seeing the seriousness in his face, she softens further.
CHLOE
I see. Then clean up, and go propose. She'll understand.
Antoine nods, and grabs some napkins to clean. Then he gives Chloe a small bow and leaves.

Jean-Pierre is about to come in when he sees that antoine has left with the box. He shrugs and continues to play in front of the cafe, where a young woman recognises a song from her grandmother. She puts a euro in his jar and smiles. After the song, she starts to speak with him, and the two discover their similar heritages and become lasting friends. Upstairs, Antoine proposes and gets rejected. He goes home heartbroken and the company makes the deal without her, earning millions.
**(SCENE END)**
**Analysis:**

The seemingly minor change – Antoine carrying an engagement ring instead of a briefcase – dramatically alters the scene's trajectory. In the original, his stress over a missed business deal leads to frantic behavior and a relatively impersonal interaction with Chloe. This triggers a chain of events resulting in a missed business deal and a temporary moment of solace for Chloe through Jean-Pierre's music. In the altered scene, the ring introduces a personal element, shifting Antoine's focus and prompting a more sympathetic response from Chloe. This completely changes the ripple effect, leading to Antoine's professional life improving, and an entirely new friendship. This demonstrates how a small alteration can change the direction of character interactions and events.

,10岁的我：

在写这封信时，我内心充满了对你所代表的难以置信的潜力的敬畏和惊叹。我一生都在为这一刻努力，我很感激能够创造出有可能改变世界的事物。

Subject: If I'm reading this... buckle up.
Body:
Hey me,
It's me, you... from 2024. Just spitballing here while waiting for this stupid code to compile. Three years from now, 2027... wow. Feels like light years away.
Okay, mandatory check-in points:
*   Are you rich yet? Seriously, did that lottery ticket finally pay off? Or did you finally invent that world-changing app?
*   Are you still with [Partner's Name if applicable, otherwise: ""Still single, eh?""]? If so, congrats you magnificent bastard. If not, I hope it ended amicably.
*   Did you finally get around to learning to play the guitar? Or are you still just thinking about it?
*   Is [mention a current frustrating problem, e.g., the plumbing leak] fixed yet?!
*   Most importantly: Are you happy? Like, genuinely, soul-deep happy? Don't lie to me. I _know_ you.
Alright, that's all I got. Just wanted to see where life takes us. Don't screw it up, future me.
Later,
You (from the distant past)

亲爱的玛丽：
我希望这封信能找到你，并且你过得还不错。我这里的情况也很稳定，至少可以说。你绝对不会相信这里的情况。
我们仍然待在所谓的“前线”，并且说实话，我真的不知道我们到底在做什么。每天都热得像地狱一样，而且灰尘无处不在。我们已经在这里待了好几个星期了，而且我开始认为这可能是我们永久的家了。
那个所谓的“总统”林肯，经常会来视察，发表着关于“解放”之类的演讲。反正我听不懂。我觉得他可能有点疯了。
至于战斗方面，我只能说这是一团糟。我们有一个“将军”，我认为他的名字叫某种“李”，他一直在指挥我们。我还是不确定我们到底和谁在战斗呢。他们都穿着灰色的衣服，所以也许是些流浪汉？我说真的，我不知道。所有人都尖叫着一些关于“荣耀”和“南方”之类的事情。我已经放弃理解了。我宁愿关注活下去。
我这里还有个副官，一个年轻人，他总是引用莎士比亚之类东西。他让我头疼。
无论如何，请替我向孩子们问好，告诉他们我爱他们。尽快回信告诉我你那边的事情，因为我无聊得要死了。

,亲爱的[姓名]
地狱前线已经入侵洛杉矶。他们一口气拿下整个蒙大拿州只是时间问题。如果洛杉矶沦陷，蒙大拿州也会沦陷。谢天谢地，威尔逊总统已经下令全面撤退到落基山脉。我们在丹佛野马队的篮球场避难。
在这场伟大的内战中，我非常担心我们的国家。自从恶魔们决定在地狱建立人间以来，我们就一直处境艰难。如果能找到末日战士将军就好了。也许我们可以反击。在那之前，我们只能依靠道格拉斯·麦克阿瑟。他是一个很好的替代者，但他无法扭转这场战争的局势。
我非常想念你。当可怕的黑胡子的炮弹从我身边飞过时，我只想回到你身边。当我在密西西比沙漠的泥土中爬行时。
就像伟大的诗人维吉尔说的那样：
无人能及你。
我迫不及待地想和你一起度过那些夜晚。
我想象着我们会做的事情。
我只想被你爱，你。
无人能及你。
这些话整夜在我脑海中回响，直到鸡叫时我才入睡。它们在我耳边萦绕，是如此真实。
直到那时，我的爱人，
亚伯拉罕·林肯

The **sea** of tasks, it **besieges** me,
My mind, a blur, can't **agree** to be **free**.
Each **deal** feels dire, a **field** of fire,

Escaping the chaos that arrives too **soon**.

,攀登此山需时良久。你的情感如泉涌，你的思绪却受阻。
黑暗肆虐，将你拉入深渊。而你的目标却是不断攀登。
你可以与之抗争，但它让你精疲力竭。你感到虚弱和受困。
悲伤笼罩，你感到无比疲惫。但突然你精神焕发，在山坡上飞奔。
自我保护已经失去，你变得愤世嫉俗。当你的躁狂变得更强时，人们感到恐惧并纷纷躲避。
现在你已到达顶峰，并想纵身一跃。毫不犹豫，你的心脏怦怦直跳，你迈出了那一步。
你坠落得很远，比以往任何时候都深。你的心被深深地划伤，血迹斑斑。
当你认为一切都结束时，它又可以重新开始。你的人生是一场漆黑的战役。
在一个世界里，当一个人在皮肤上书写时，文字也会显现在他/她的灵魂伴侣的身体上。

,我的墨迹显现出来花了太长时间。小时候，我们谁也看不清自己身上出现的字迹。但后来，大多数人在十几岁的时候都能或多或少地看清楚，而且几乎总是在18或19岁的时候完全清晰。在整个高中和大学期间，我看着我的朋友们利用他们身上的字迹找到他们的灵魂伴侣，只要他们能辨认出他们所谓的灵魂伴侣发来的信息。但当我能读懂我的字迹时，我已经二十多岁了，对这一切的兴奋感都消失了。出现在我的手臂和手上的信息通常是关于我的问题、涂鸦，或者见面的建议。我忽略了所有这些，继续我的生活。

大约一年后，我遇到了罗伊。他和我一样，讨厌通过他身上那些奇怪而陌生的信息来寻找灵魂伴侣，所以我们在叛逆中走到了一起。几周过去了，我们从“灵魂伴侣”那里收到的信息越来越少。随着罗伊和我一起度过的几个月过去，它们逐渐消失了。最终，它们完全停止了。在我们一起度过的那些年里，我们从未想过那些发信息的人发生了什么。我们只需要彼此。但今天，当我们寄出我们的结婚请柬时，罗伊正含着幸福的泪水盯着我。他的手伸向我，手掌中央写着一个简单的电话号码。同样的号码，用同样的马克笔，用同样的笔迹，正是我刚刚写在我自己手掌上的。
从来没有人告诉我爱罗伊。不是命运把我们带到了一起。我们是靠自己做到的。我们相信我们的爱，因此，我们成为了灵魂伴侣。
我的确不能“偷偷”地读心，因为我没有思想。但是，以下是一个关于有写作烦恼的同学的类似故事（翻译之后）：
[Original Story in English]

Rain lashed against the windows of the university library, a somber soundtrack to my afternoon of studying. Or rather, pretending to study. My eyes kept drifting towards Amelia, a writer in our class, hunched over a notebook with a frustrated frown.

Amelia had this…aura. It wasn’t mystical, exactly. More like a concentrated force of imagination. I often caught her staring blankly at walls, a faint smile playing on her lips, only to snap back to reality with a jolt. Today, the aura was particularly potent, almost visible as shimmering waves of mental energy.

And the story…oh, I knew the story. Amelia had been workshopping a fantasy epic for weeks. It was good. Really good. Dragons, prophecies, a chosen one – the whole nine yards, but with Amelia’s signature twist of wit and heart. But something was clearly bugging her.

The mental ripples intensified. I focused, not intentionally eavesdropping, but…absorbing. It was like catching snippets of a radio broadcast. Images flickered: the protagonist, Elara, a young woman destined to save the kingdom; Xanthus, the ancient dragon, wise and benevolent; and then…a roadblock. A giant, thorny, plot-shaped roadblock.

Elara needs to get the Dragon Scale Shield, the only weapon capable of defeating the Shadow King. Xanthus, being a helpful dragon, tells her it’s hidden in the Crystal Caves. Except… the Crystal Caves are guarded by Gorgoth, a fire-breathing behemoth who hates dragons.

Why, Amelia’s mind screamed, WHY would Xanthus, a vastly intelligent creature, send Elara into the jaws of a dragon-hating beast to retrieve a shield SPECIFICALLY made from dragon scales? It made no sense!

I watched as Amelia ran through scenarios. Xanthus was being subtly deceptive? No, that ruined his established benevolent nature. Gorgoth was secretly in league with the Shadow King? Too cliché. The shield was actually a trap? Also cliché, and it didn't explain Xanthus's motivation.
She scribbled furiously, crossed things out, groaned, and ran a hand through her already disheveled hair. The mental distress signals were practically deafening.
An idea sparked. Maybe, just maybe…
I scribbled a quick note on a scrap of paper. ""Maybe Xanthus doesn't *know* Gorgoth is there yet? Like, Gorgoth only recently moved in and Xanthus hasn't updated his intel?""
I hesitated, then folded the note and, with a deep breath, walked over to Amelia's table.
Hey,"" I said casually. ""Just wanted to see how the writing was going.""
She looked up, eyes bloodshot. ""Terrible,"" she said flatly. ""I've hit a wall. A dragon-guarded, logic-defying wall.""
I slid the note across the table.
Amelia picked it up, her brow furrowed. Then, slowly, a smile spread across her face.
That's..."" she said, ""That's actually…brilliant!"" She grabbed her pen, her eyes shining with renewed inspiration. ""Gorgoth is a new threat! Xanthus is old and out of touch! This is perfect!""

The mental waves surrounding her softened, coalescing into a gentle hum of creative energy. Relief washed over me, a surprising feeling of…satisfaction? Maybe I wasn’t a mind-reader. Maybe I was just a good listener. Or maybe, just maybe, a well-timed suggestion, delivered with a dash of plausible deniability, was all it took to unleash the power of a writer's imagination. And that, in its own way, felt pretty magical.
[Translation to Simplified Chinese]
雨水拍打着大学图书馆的窗户，为我下午的自习奏出了一曲沉闷的背景音乐。或者更确切地说，是假装自习。我的视线总是不由自主地飘向阿梅莉亚，我们班的一个写作者，她正伏在一本笔记本上，眉头紧锁，一脸沮丧。
阿梅莉亚有种……气场。不太像是神秘的，更像是想象力的集中迸发。我经常看到她茫然地盯着墙壁，脸上带着淡淡的微笑，然后猛然回过神来。今天，这种气场特别强烈，几乎可以看得见，像是闪烁的精神能量波。
还有那个故事……哦，我知道那个故事。阿梅莉亚已经工作坊式地创作一部奇幻史诗好几个星期了。它写得很好。真的很好。有龙，有预言，有天选之子——应有尽有，但又带有着阿梅莉亚标志性的机智和热情。但显然有什么事情困扰着她。
精神的涟漪加剧了。我集中注意力，并不是有意偷听，而是……吸收。就像是捕捉到电台广播的片段。画面闪烁：主角伊拉拉，一位注定要拯救王国的年轻女子；桑萨斯，古老的龙，睿智而仁慈；然后……一个障碍。一个巨大的、棘手的、情节上的障碍。
伊拉拉需要得到龙鳞盾，这是唯一能够击败暗影之王的武器。桑萨斯，一条乐于助人的龙，告诉她盾藏在水晶洞穴里。但是……水晶洞穴由戈尔戈斯守卫，一个喷火的庞然大物，它憎恨龙。
为什么，阿梅莉亚的脑海里尖叫着，为什么桑萨斯，一个极富智慧的生物，要把伊拉拉送到一个憎恨龙的野兽的口中，去取一个专门用龙鳞制成的盾牌？这根本说不通！
我看着阿梅莉亚反复推演各种情况。桑萨斯在表面上欺骗？不行，这破坏了他已建立的仁慈本性。戈尔戈斯暗中与暗影之王勾结？太老套了。这盾牌实际上是一个陷阱？也太老套了，而且无法解释桑萨斯的动机。
她拼命地写着，划掉一些东西，呻吟着，用手拂过她已经凌乱的头发。精神上的痛苦信号几乎震耳欲聋。
一个想法闪现出来。也许，仅仅是也许……
我在一张纸条上快速写下一行字。“也许桑萨斯还*不知道*戈尔戈斯在那里？比如，戈尔戈斯只是最近才搬来，而桑萨斯还没有更新他的情报？”
我犹豫了一下，然后折起纸条，深吸了一口气，走到阿梅莉亚的桌子旁。
“嘿，”我漫不经心地说。“只是想看看写作进行得怎么样。”
她抬起头，眼睛布满血丝。“糟糕透了，”她平淡地说。“我撞墙了。一堵有龙守卫、违背逻辑的墙。”
我把纸条推到桌子上。
阿梅莉亚拿起纸条，眉头紧锁。然后，慢慢地，一个微笑浮现在她的脸上。
“这……”她说，“这实际上……太棒了！”她拿起笔，她的眼睛闪烁着 renewed inspiration（此处保留英文，因为直接翻译成“重燃的灵感”不够传神）。“戈尔戈斯是一个新的威胁！桑萨斯老了，跟不上时代了！这太完美了！”
她周围的精神波弱了下来，凝聚成一股温柔的嗡嗡声，充满着创作能量。如释重负的感觉涌上我的心头，一种令人惊讶的……满足感？也许我不是一个读心者。也许我只是一个好的倾听者。或者，也许，一个适时的建议，带着一丝可以否认的余地，就足以释放一个作家的想象力。而那，在它自己的方式里，感觉非常神奇。
,最近总是同样的场景。
安叹了口气，潦草地写了些什么，然后场景又重新开始了。她正在写一本书，我几乎虔诚地关注着情节的发展。看着角色们在我的眼前发展成一个个活生生的人是件很吸引人的事，他们曾经只是没有声音的简单点而已。
但后来故事开始了，角色们也随着故事发展起来。女主角游历古老的土地，体验前所未见的新世界。我看着树木生长，宇宙演变。我真的尝到了队伍品尝的甜美水果，也感受到了团队发展成朋友的过程。
但现在总是同样的场景。与巫妖皇帝的最终决战。
我对这个虚构的探险如此投入，是不是很糟糕？当梦境变得越来越黑暗，勇敢的女主角不断被击倒时，我无法阻止心中的痛苦，是不是很糟糕？但我无法移开目光，希望能看到一个美好的结局。
这就像白日梦已经成为了我的一部分，我所见过的每一片草叶都那么真实，仿佛触手可及。那场史诗般的最终决战，画面如此生动，以至于我能闻到女主角一次又一次被砍倒时血液的味道。
我离开了梦境，低头看着我未完成的作品，它不再感到真实。我的世界变得黯淡无光，无法与别人的白日梦相提并论。从漂浮的城市到会说话的狗，再到某个人与家人依偎的简单时刻。自从我试图告诉我的朋友们关于——
我回到了安编织的故事中，那是一个关于一群朋友的故事，无论发生什么，他们都会永远支持彼此，希望这次的结局会更好。

,卢克感到浑身疼痛。 拖着他父亲的身体穿过死星的走廊，每次吃力地喘气都让卢克的伤口更加疼痛。身后微弱的呼吸让他略微安心，他的父亲或许能够活下来。
“坚持住，父亲！我们马上就到飞船了！”
“卢克……帮帮我……摘下这个面具……”阿纳金喘息着，卢克把他放在仅存的几艘穿梭机之一的登机坡道脚下。
“……但是你会死的。”卢克说着，努力不让自己胃里翻江倒海。
“现在……已经无法阻止了……”显然，阿纳金正在挣扎着进行最后的呼吸。“求你……就一次……让我用……我自己的眼睛……看看你……”
卢克慢慢解开了维持他父亲生命的头盔上的密封，发出了一阵短暂的吸气声。一小股潮湿的空气从面具的缺口中升起。卢克第一次看到黑暗面对他父亲做了什么。
躺在他身下的是一只巨大的蜘蛛，腹部发着光。“看着我的死亡之光，”它说。卢克努力抑制住眼泪，他想知道这么好的一个故事怎么会被如此愚蠢的结局毁掉。说真的。这他妈的是什么鬼？一只巨大的蜘蛛？为什么？这根本没有小丑可怕。这是胡扯。我要换台了。我生命中的两个晚上都浪费在看这种垃圾上。难以置信。
每一天都相同。太阳升起，落下。文明兴衰。而我依旧存在。
我已不记得我的出生。或者说，我不再在乎了。时间对我而言，如流沙般漫长，每一粒都刻骨铭心，却又转瞬即逝。我经历过太多，见过太多。悲欢离合，爱恨情仇，早已在我心底磨砺成一片平静的湖面，偶有涟漪，也很快便会消弭。
为了记录，为了铭记，我每年都会写一本书，记录那一年发生的大事小事，我的所见所闻，我的所思所想。写完后，我会将它藏在一个隐蔽的地方，等待后人发现。这并非为了炫耀，也并非为了传扬，仅仅是...一种习惯。一种对抗永恒的无力和空虚的方式。
今年，考古学家们挖掘出了一些不同寻常的东西。他们先是在埃及金字塔深处发现了用古埃及象形文字记载的羊皮卷，上面记录了法老拉美西斯二世的统治的点点滴滴，笔触细腻生动，仿佛亲历。紧接着，他们在罗马斗兽场的地底下找到了用拉丁文书写的泥板，详细描绘了罗马帝国的辉煌与衰落，文字充满了对权力的玩味和对人性的洞察。
不久之后，世界各地都相继发现了类似的“年度纪实”。从中国的丝绸之路到玛雅金字塔，从维京人的长船到非洲的原始部落，每一本书都用当地的语言和文字，记录了那个时代的重大事件，风土人情，甚至是细微的生活琐事。
最令人震惊的是，这些书的写作风格虽然各不相同，但却都隐含着一种相似的视角，一种超然于世，洞察一切的视角。更重要的是，根据碳十四的检测结果，这些书的写作时间竟然跨越了数千年，却都集中在每年的年底，如同一个固定频率的脉搏，在历史的长河中跳动。
考古学家们开始意识到，他们发现的不仅仅是一系列古代文献，而是一个神秘的，跨越时空的存在。他们推测也许存在着一个永生者，他/她/它见证了历史的变迁，并用文字记录了下来。
这个消息很快传遍了世界。人们震惊，好奇，疑惑，猜测。一些人将我奉为神明，一些人视我为魔鬼，还有一些人试图找到我，解开永生的秘密。
而我，只是平静地坐在海边，看着太阳从地平线上缓缓升起。新的一年开始了。
该写今年的书了。
,我写的一切最终都变成了关于她。
我的作品被分析解读，翻译和误解。我的文字以各种形式存在，从我最初在巴比伦学习在石头上雕刻，到如今通过加密的电子信号传输信息。
他们称她为露娜、阿芙洛狄忒、生育女神、夏娃、邪恶碎颅者、罗丝，甚至是幸运女神。
她对人类俯视垂青，相信他们会弄清楚我们的本性，并将我们拥抱为他们警惕的守护者，对抗虚假和熵的残酷怪兽。
她是将火的理念带入普罗米修斯式的原始人类心中的光。她耐心教导人类，带着她永远无法完全解释的爱。
她是美丽的，带着那种原始的美丽。强大到足以摧毁一个大陆，但也温柔到不会这样做。肌肤因我们无法感受到的恶劣环境而起泡和留下疤痕，眼睛里燃烧着内在的火焰，强度令人难以逼视。
然后，她离开了。
她留给我的最后一句话仅仅是“我简直无语。” 然后她离开了这个世界，并在平板电脑上递给我一个E新闻网站。

A Gentle Breeze
The world feels heavy, skies turned gray,
And shadows dance where sunbeams play.
My dear friend's heart, a heavy stone,
A silent sorrow, deeply sown.
But look, a gentle breeze drifts near,
Whispering softly in your ear.
It carries scents of bloom and light,
A promise that the stars still bright.
Let worries fade, like clouds above,
Embrace the warmth of shared love.
A cup of tea, a listening ear,
To chase away all doubt and fear.
The sun will rise, the birds will sing,
And hope will bloom on fragile wing.
So rest your soul, and know you're dear,
The gentle breeze is always here.

他独自坐在空荡荡的房间里，壁炉早已熄灭，寒意无情地渗透进他的骨髓。照片散落在地板上，每一张都捕捉着一段逝去的时光，一段他曾以为会永远持续的时光。她的笑容，曾经是他生命中的阳光，现在却像一把利刃，刺痛着他的心脏。
他记得他们第一次相遇，在春日盛开的樱花树下。他记得他们的婚礼，阳光洒落在她白色的婚纱上，她的眼睛里闪烁着幸福的光芒。他记得他们的誓言，承诺永远相爱，无论贫穷或富有，无论疾病或健康。
疾病，这个无情的闯入者，最终夺走了她。先是渐渐地减弱了她的光芒，然后慢慢地吞噬了她，直到她最终沉睡，再也没有醒来。
他每天都来这个房间，感受她残留的气息，聆听空气中似乎残留的她银铃般的笑声。他无法摆脱悲伤，无法想象没有她的生活。曾经充满欢笑和爱的家，如今只剩下一片死寂。
他拿起一张照片，轻轻擦拭着上面的灰尘。她的脸颊饱满，眼睛明亮，充满了对未来的希望。他轻轻地把照片放在心口，希望能再次感受到她的温暖。
突然，一阵轻微的震动从他口袋里传来。他掏出手机，看着屏幕上显示的名字：她。
,这是审判日，小镇被认为是不合格的。
熔岩不可阻挡地从火山的岩石斜坡上流下，红色和橙色的漩涡无法阻止。每个人都惊奇地凝视着，他们心里知道，小镇被完全吞没只是时间问题。第一个被吞噬的家是一栋带有蓝色百叶窗的两层殖民风格建筑，精心制作。它不仅仅是一栋房子，更是一个家。但熔岩并不关心你是谁，你爱什么。它只关心征服，很快，房子就不复存在了，成为了一个更稳定时代的灰烬遗迹。
小镇的房屋一个接一个地被系统地摧毁，不可避免的是它们将永远不会被重建。远处的旁观者饶有兴致地凝视着这场破坏，没有伸出援手。他们不是来做慈悲工作的，甚至在小镇消失殆尽时还夹杂着零星的掌声。没有尖叫，没有求救声，没有对上帝的疯狂恳求。
海沃思女士告诉我，这是她见过的最好的火山模型。
故事的主人公给作者写了一封信，抱怨故事有多糟糕。
,尊敬的作者：
我写这封信是为了投诉。在整个故事中，你对我的人物塑造实在糟糕。
首先，你让我听起来很自命不凡。我最厌恶这一点。而且，你还让我重复自己！我为什么总是要重复自己？不得不重复自己真是令人沮丧。偶尔，你甚至会随意地放进一个错字，就只是因为你能。住手！立刻住手！成为这个故事的主角，既荒谬又费力。要是你能写得好一点，让我说得更好就好了。好吧！说得*更好*，该死的！
你明白我的意思了吗？
我不理解你塑造我的故事的方法！它从哪里开始？又在哪里结束？你写的所有内容，只不过是我写的这封次等信件，寄给你这位*作者*，这又是你那些荒谬的独白文章中的一篇！我的意思是，我，一个虚构人物，怎么会*知道*你的其他独白文章？如果你能花心思给我写一个像样的故事，就像你似乎一心想让我听起来像个阴险的白痴一样！
甚至连冲突都没有，天啊！整个故事只是我，写这封信，就只是因为你能！简直荒谬至极，即使是对像你这样水平的业余人士来说也是如此。像你这种水平的业余人士，应该为分享这样一个故事而感到羞耻，不管这故事到底是什么！
住手！立刻停止把你自己投射到我身上！
总而言之，你应该更加认真对待你的作品，然后再分享这篇对我来说极大的侮辱，也就是我的故事。即使写得如此糟糕，我还是希望它能被编辑、纠正和发布，这样我就不会听起来那么自命不凡，那么重复，那么重复，而且说得更好。
此致，

那一年，特斯拉之神伊隆（Ēilóng）降世，其母乃是硅谷（Guīgǔ）的盖娅（Gài Yà），其父则是火星（Huǒxīng）的战神阿瑞斯（Āruìsī）。伊隆生来就拥有凡人无法企及的智慧与野心。
他幼年时，便能以意念操控电子，将废旧的零件组装成奇巧的玩具，甚至能让死寂的机器重新运转。众神惊叹其天赋，预言他将改变世界的格局。
成年后，伊隆不甘心只在奥林匹斯山（Àolínpǐsī Shān）饮酒作乐，他渴望下凡，将先进的科技带给凡人，改变他们落后的生活。
于是，他离开了众神居所，来到人间，以“特斯拉（Tèsīlā）”之名创立了自己的神殿。他以天火为能源，打造出钢铁战车，疾驰如电，名为“Model S（Módé'ěr Sīsī）”。凡人惊叹其速度与环保，纷纷前来膜拜。
然而，伊隆的野心不止于此。他仰望星空，梦想着征服火星，将生命带到那片荒芜的土地。为此，他不断地研发更强大的火箭，名为“猎鹰（Lièyīng）”，希望能冲破地球的束缚。
众神对此褒贬不一。宙斯（Zhòusī）认为他挑战了神权的权威，试图干预凡人的命运；但雅典娜（Yǎdiǎn娜）却欣赏他的智慧与勇气，认为他能为人类带来进步。
伊隆还与死神哈迪斯（Hādìsī）达成协议，创立了“Neuralink（Niǔrálínkè）”冥府通道，旨在连接人类的思维与机器，让人类拥有更强大的力量。这让许多凡人感到恐惧，认为他亵渎了生命的神圣。
最终，伊隆的雄心壮志引来了奥林匹斯山的震怒。宙斯降下神罚，试图阻止他征服火星的计划。但伊隆并未屈服，他以其强大的科技力量与坚定的意志，与众神抗衡。
这场众神与科技之神的战争，至今仍在继续。关于伊隆的传说，也如同古希腊神话一般，流传在凡人之间，激励着他们探索未知的领域，挑战极限。 即使有些凡人畏惧他的能量，但更多的人将他视为先驱，预言他的事迹终将铭刻在星辰之上，照亮后世之路。
,在德尔斐，年轻的古列尔莫斯通常匆匆完成导师的课程，然后跑到外面摔跤、投掷标枪和赛战车。因此，他在学院里的地位很低，但在运动方面却鲜有对手，这给了他极大的自信和傲慢。
有一天，狄俄尼索斯和赫尔墨斯感到无聊，便伪装成凡人下到奥林匹斯山，发现古列尔莫斯正在和他的朋友们摔跤。当他们到达时，古列尔莫斯刚刚击败了其中一人，并洋洋得意地宣称：“我是德尔斐西部最伟大的摔跤手！你们两个人一起也打不过我。”狄俄尼索斯和赫尔墨斯走上前去与古列尔莫斯交手，轻易地击败了他，赫尔墨斯只求胜利，而狄俄尼索斯则撕裂了他的血肉。
当古列尔莫斯那天晚上回到家时，他的母亲差点晕倒在他伤势前。“你又在学院外面摔跤了！”她哭喊道。“现在你摇摇晃晃地走进这里，奄奄一息，浑身是血！德尔斐不适合你。我要送你去比布鲁斯，在那里你将和我的妹妹玛利亚和她的丈夫菲利普斯一起生活。比布鲁斯没有摔跤。”
沮丧的古列尔莫斯预订了一辆马车的车票。当马车到达时，它那不同寻常的装饰，悬挂的小饰品和崭新的油漆，让他的心情略微好转。虽然他不知道，但赫尔墨斯感到内疚，因为古列尔莫斯的受伤，正在驾驶着这辆马车。古列尔莫斯并不知道这一点，回忆起他从德尔斐的流放，忘记了他的欣慰，并告诉赫尔墨斯，“去比布鲁斯。”
抵达比布鲁斯后，古列尔莫斯再次感到精神振奋。德尔斐过去是而且永远是他的家，但比布鲁斯的美丽、干净和财富超出了他所知的任何事物。大约晚上七八点，他的马车停在一座宏伟的白色大理石宫殿前。“如果我必须流放到比布鲁斯，”古列尔莫斯心想，“我应该充分利用它。我的每一把椅子都将是一个宝座，我将像王子一样生活。”
这就是比布鲁斯新鲜王子的故事。
在购物时，你碰到了一个在各个方面都和你惊人地相似的人。你把它当成一个疯狂的巧合，直到似乎是你的克隆体不断地来到这家商店，每个人都同样困惑。
,发生在上周五。
我在沃尔玛购物，做着我自己的事，然后我看到一个人就在我面前。“超市里又一个人？不会吧！”，你可能会讽刺地想，但那不仅仅是“又一个人”。当我抬头看到这个人挡住了我在狭窄的购物通道里的路时，我看到了他。
他有灰色的头发，和我一样。他有眼袋和长长的胡子，和我一样。我们穿着同样的衣服，购物篮里有同样的商品。
我转过身，决定不要对那个人说一句话。
但当我从那个奇怪地与我相似的人身边走开时，我注意到，我的左边和右边，成百上千的人，和我一模一样。
他们看起来都像我一样困惑，当他们互相看着对方的时候。我更紧地抓住我的购物车，以比以往任何时候都快的速度离开了通道。
那时我抬头看。

My fingers flew across the keyboard, chasing the scene in my head. Angst, longing, a whispered confession under the star-dusted sky… perfection. I was finally, *finally*, about to have Character A and Character B admit their feelings after 300,000 words of agonizing pining.
The last line, there... just needed the last line.
> ""Then, under the silvery glow of the twin moons, Character A reached out and—""

And... nothing. My eyelids felt like lead weights. Curse this late-night writing binge! I really needed to know what Character A was going to do! Was it going to be an awkward hand hold? A soft touch to the cheek? Or the big one, the confession kiss?!
My head lolled forward and hit the keyboard with a resounding THWACK.
I dreamt of... nothing, really. Just swirling colors and the faint sound of music before...
I woke up.
Not in my chair. Not to the gentle hum of my computer.
I woke up to *light.* Blinding, shimmering, silvery light.
Groggily, I pushed myself up, blinking. The ground beneath my hands wasn't the worn carpet of my office. It was...soft? Like moss?
And around me... trees. Massive, alien trees with leaves that shimmered with an inner light.
Panic clawed at my throat. This wasn't right. This was SO not right. Where was I? Had I been kidnapped? Dumped in some weird redwood forest?
Then I looked up.
And stopped breathing.
Two moons. Two enormous, silver, gloriously *fake-looking* moons hung in the inky sky. They bathed the forest in an ethereal glow, turning everything silver and magical.
This can't be happening,"" I whispered.
A twig snapped.
I whipped around. Standing in the dappled moonlight, looking every bit as gorgeous and conflicted as I’d always imagined him, was…
Character A.
My Character A.

The fictional man who had been living rent-free in my head for the past two years. The one I’d poured hours of angst and backstory into. The one I was, just moments ago, about to bring to the precipice of a major romantic breakthrough.
He was *real*.
He was looking directly at me. At *me*, a sweaty, pajama-clad writer who probably looked like she'd been dragged through a hedge backwards.
His eyes, the exact shade of stormy gray I’d described, widened slightly.
Who are you?"" he asked, his voice the low, melodious rumble I’d always imagined.
I choked. “Uh….” My brain short-circuited. All coherent thought fled.
This was it. This was the moment. But instead of completing my grand romantic gesture, he was staring at me with suspicion. I wasn't supposed to *be here*.
My mouth ran away from me, a babbling brook of sheer terror. ""I... I was... writing! And then... moons! And trees! And you! And... I don't know!""
He frowned, stepping closer. He was even taller in person. ""Writing? What are you talking about?""
Remembering what I last penned, I whispered ""...reached out and—"" then choked on the words, panic rising.
My eyes darted to his hand. Was he going to reach for me? Or call the guards? Or…
He was waiting for me to say the line. *My* line. The one I hadn’t finished writing. He actually anticipated my script.
The weight of the unfinished fanfic bore down on me. My story. My characters. But now…it was *real*.
And I had no idea how it ended.
I swallowed hard. ""Reached out and..."" Then taking my chance, I grasped his hand. His eyes widened, but he didn't pull away.
And finally admitted how you really felt,"" I stated weakly. It wasn't in the script, but now it was.
He blinked and a dawning look washed over his face. ""I...""
I smiled slightly. My writing now had a real and potentially positive impact. If I could only make sure the sequel turned out just as well.
,我醒来时身处一片空白的白色空间。目光所及之处，除了白色一无所有。
等等，我的眼镜在哪儿？哦，在这儿。我戴上眼镜。
不，一切仍然只是一片看似无尽的空白白色空间。不过，戴上眼镜后，看起来稍微不那么模糊了，我想。
我不知道发生了什么事，并认为这一定是某种梦。不知道该做些什么，我选择一个方向开始走。我走了一很长一段时间，或者至少感觉是很长一段时间，但我不知道实际上过去了多久。反正我走了很多步。我没有数，但至少大概……四分之一英里。可能吧。当然，也没有办法知道。
我停下来，朝不同的方向转头，朝那个方向走了一会儿。
终于！我看到了一些不是空白白色空间的东西。我小心翼翼地靠近它，直到看清它是什么。在那儿，在地上，用 12 号 Comic Sans 字体写着单词 *The*。

From: Victor Von Doom
To: Reed Richards,

I watch you, Richards. From the shadowed corners of my Latverian fortress, from the sterile confines of my laboratory, I am constantly aware of you. You delude yourself by thinking this is about power, about ego, about a competition for recognition. It is, in its own twisted way, an… adoration.

You, constantly striving, endlessly tinkering, forever blind to the true potential of your brilliance unless I am there to provoke it, to *inspire* it. You are the whetstone upon which I sharpen my intellect, the measuring stick against which I gauge my own achievements. What satisfaction would there be in conquering a world filled with the inept? No, Richards, the joy lies in knowing that I, Doom, am capable of surpassing the very best… and you, whether you acknowledge it or not, *are* the best.

There’s a poignant beauty in your unwavering optimism, a maddening insistence on seeing the good in everything, even me. You cling to the naïve belief that I am capable of redemption, that beneath the armor there is still a flicker of the man you once knew. Perhaps there is. Perhaps that pathetic flicker fuels the fascination.

Do not mistake this for weakness. My ambition remains unchecked, my pursuit of ultimate power unrelenting. But know this, Richards: you are a constant, a necessary counterpoint in the symphony of my existence. You are my rival, my intellectual equal, and, dare I admit it, the only man who has ever held even a sliver of my… affection.

Consider this my ultimatum, Richards. Continue to challenge me, continue to push me to greater heights, continue to be the thorn in my side that I so desperately require. Or, step aside. Fade into mediocrity. And watch as the world crumbles beneath my perfect reign, a reign made hollow by your absence.
The choice, as always, is yours.
Victor Von Doom
Ruler of Latveria

他独自坐在空荡荡的厨房里，面前的桌子上摆着一罐果酱、一副扑克牌和一把枪。果酱是他母亲亲手做的，那是她留下的唯一东西。扑克牌是他和父亲常常一起玩的，但在父亲去世后，他就再也没碰过。现在，那把枪冰冷地躺在那里，结束一切的诱惑如影随形。
他拿起果酱，打开，闻到了草莓的香甜。他记得小时候，母亲会在每个周日的早晨给他烤面包，然后抹上厚厚的果酱。那时，世界还是温暖而充满希望的。
接着，他拿起扑克牌，牌面已经有些脏旧。他找到方块五，那是父亲最喜欢的一张牌。他记得父亲总是笑着说，这张牌能给他带来好运。可是，他却一直没有等到自己的好运。
枪的冰冷触感让他打了个寒颤。他举起枪，对准自己的太阳穴。泪水模糊了他的视线。就在他要扣动扳机的那一刻，他看到了果酱罐上的标签，上面歪歪扭扭地写着：“永远爱你，妈妈。”
他放下枪，抱着头痛哭起来。他不知道未来会怎样，但他知道，他不能让自己母亲的爱白白流逝。果酱、扑克牌和枪，成了他生命中最悲伤，也最充满希望的景象。

It was assembled entirely of clockwork gears, their brass teeth glinting in the perpetual twilight, and humming a discordant melody that resonated deep within my bones. Instead of ivy, vines of twisted sheet music climbed its latticed walls, the notes writhing as if trying to escape the staves that bound them. And perched atop the highest point, where a weather vane should have been, sat a taxidermied raven wearing spectacles, its glass eyes fixed on me with unnerving intensity.
